job.title: Matchday Retail Sales Assistant
job.location: Selhurst Park Stadium, London, London, SE25 6PU, GB
job.html_description:
Job Location:      Selhurst Park Stadium, SE25 6PU

Contract Type:   0-hour, Fixed-Term Contract

Salary/ ROP:       £13.15


Who we are:

We are Crystal Palace Football Club, a Premier League Football Club with more than 160 years of proud history. Spread across three different sites - brand new main offices at Selhurst Park Stadium, our London office, and our Training Ground/Academy, we offer the best atmosphere and culture for our fans and our employees. 


We strive for excellence in the workplace with a philosophy of high standards, professionalism, and a strong work ethic from our employees. We are constantly trying to grow, diversify and build with not only our team on the pitch, but also our team in the offices and on the ground.

About this role:

This role involves supporting the day-to-day operations of the Club’s retail store, delivering excellent customer service, maintaining high standards of presentation, and ensuring efficient stock handling and sales processes.


Responsibilities:

  • Providing a high level of customer service on the shop floor

  • Operating the till accurately and efficiently

  • Packing and processing customer purchases

  • Replenishing and merchandising stock on shelves as required

  • Pricing and labeling merchandise correctly

  • Printing names and numbers on shirts to required specifications

  • Organising and maintaining merchandise within store rooms

  • Receiving, checking, and storing deliveries

  • Other duties and responsibilities as required by line manager.

  • Upholding and promoting the Club’s policies and procedures, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Equal Opportunities policies and procedures.

Experience, Skills and Qualifications

  • Previous experience working in a retail environment

  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills

  • Strong time management and organisational abilities

  • Ability to work effectively under pressure and meet deadlines

  • High attention to detail

Enthusiastic, proactive, and positive approach to all tasks


Our commitment to Equality

At Crystal Palace Football Club, Equality, Diversity & Inclusion is spirited and continues to grow due to the working environment, which makes all staff feel appreciated and welcomed. Crystal Palace Football Club do this by encouraging good relations and practices towards different groups and are committed to promoting employment opportunities that celebrate diversity.

We are committed to attracting, recruiting, and retaining the most talented candidates and the club intends to ensure that everyone who wishes to engage with the club, whether as matchday fans, staff, players, board members, participants in Foundation programmes and any other person engaged with the club's activities, has a real and equal opportunity to do so.

If you require disability-related adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact DLO@cpfc.co.uk (DLO inbox is for disability-related queries only, if you wish to gain further information regarding this vacancy, please contact cpfc.recruitment@cpfc.co.uk). We are here to help you through the whole recruitment process including trying to apply for a vacancy, getting in touch with us through the process, and/or helping with access in case you are required to come in for an interview.


Safeguarding

Crystal Palace Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and vulnerable people and expects all staff to share this commitment. As such, the post holder will be required to undergo any relevant security checks, including a comprehensive background check, Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) to ensure the safety and protection of children and vulnerable individuals.

job.title: Club Events & Matchday Safeguarding Officer
job.location: Selhurst Park Stadium, London, London, SE25 6PU, GB
job.html_description:
Job Location:      Selhurst Park Stadium, SE25 6PU

Contract Type:   28 hours (permanent contract)

Deadline:            22nd March 2026

Who we are:

We are Crystal Palace Football Club, a Premier League Football Club with more than 160 years of proud history. Spread across three different sites - brand new main offices at Selhurst Park Stadium, our London office, and our Training Ground/Academy, we offer the best atmosphere and culture for our fans and our employees. 


We strive for excellence in the workplace with a philosophy of high standards, professionalism, and a strong work ethic from our employees. We are constantly trying to grow, diversify and build with not only our team on the pitch, but also our team in the offices and on the ground.

About this role:

The role is responsible for providing effective support to the Safeguarding team in relation to external matters, including MyConcern support, meetings, investigations, and providing cover when necessary. It also contributes to the successful delivery of the Club’s safeguarding mission, strategy, and policies, ensuring full compliance with Premier League standards and best practice.

In addition, the successful candidate will act as the Designated Safeguarding Officer (DSO) on matchdays, ensuring that all safeguarding protocols are consistently followed, with a focus on supporter and staff welfare and safety. The role ensures that the Premier League ‘Safer Experiences’ standards remain fully up to date and audit-ready, while providing safeguarding oversight for all events and matches at Selhurst Park Stadium, and offering support to other venues as required.

Responsibilities:

  • Working with the Head of Safeguarding to implement and promote the Club’s safeguarding policies and procedures across the Club.

  • Assisting the Safeguarding team in maintaining a safe working environment by keeping accurate and confidential records.

  • Contributing to evidencing, maintaining, evaluating and improving the Club’s Safeguarding Provision.

  • Advising others working with vulnerable groups on the implementation of organisational policies and procedures.

  • Proactively promoting and raising safeguarding awareness including the developing and delivery of relevant training, inductions and workshops to colleagues.

  • Provide safeguarding support to the U12-14 Women’s academy

  • Assisting the Head of Safeguarding to manage safeguarding concerns, investigations and referrals to football and/or statutory authorities.

  • Support and coordinate additional initiatives as directed by the Head of Safeguarding.

  • Work with the wider safeguarding team to support the Premier League audit process.

  • Act as Designated Safeguarding Officer, reporting and recording concerns in a timely manner and escalating in line with Club policy.

  • Working together in partnership with football and statutory authorities, key partners and peer organisations to share information to manage risk, to strengthen safeguarding arrangements and share good practice.

  • Coordinate the Clubs Safeguarding Champion network

  • Other duties and responsibilities as required by line manager.

  • Upholding and promoting the Club’s policies and procedures, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Equal Opportunities policies and procedures.

Match Day Responsibilities:

  • Pro-actively promote and raise the safeguarding awareness throughout the Club including on match days.

  • Designated Safeguarding Officer for match days; First port of call on match days for any safeguarding related incidents or concerns.

  • Support the management and coordinating the Ball Stewards for match days.

  • Provide oversight, support, professional advice and risk assessments for activities involving children and adults at risk, including Mascots during events and match days.

  • Follow up on all safeguarding incidents during events and match days, including supporting and liaising with spectators

  • Recruit, support and grow the network of Safeguarding Champions across the Club. Provide regular workshops and initiatives.


Experience, Skills and Qualifications:

  • Relevant and up to date safeguarding training and experience.

  • Experience working directly with vulnerable groups in a safeguarding capacity.

  • Experience in delivering training to a multitude of stakeholders.

  • Experience acting as Designated Safeguarding Officer for events or organisations.

  • Experience providing administrative support.

  • Willingness to complete relevant safeguarding training as directed.

  • An excellent communicator (including written, oral and presentation) with report writing skills.

  • Committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of vulnerable groups.

  • Approachable and have excellent interpersonal skills.

Benefits:

  • Complimentary match day ticket

  • Reward and Discount Scheme through our Tech Scheme and Simple Health app

  • Health and Wellbeing benefit scheme

  • 20% Discount in our Retail Stores

  • Discounts in various gyms through GymFlex

  • Volunteering Day - 1 Workday off to support a charity of your choice.

  • Travel Season Ticket loan

  • Holiday allowance: 25 Days 

Our commitment to Equality

At Crystal Palace Football Club, Equality, Diversity & Inclusion is spirited and continues to grow due to the working environment, which makes all staff feel appreciated and welcomed. Crystal Palace Football Club do this by encouraging good relations and practices towards different groups and are committed to promoting employment opportunities that celebrate diversity.

We are committed to attracting, recruiting, and retaining the most talented candidates and the club intends to ensure that everyone who wishes to engage with the club, whether as matchday fans, staff, players, board members, participants in Foundation programmes and any other person engaged with the club's activities, has a real and equal opportunity to do so.


Reasonable Adjustment

We are proud to be a Disability Confident Employer. We guarantee an interview to all disabled applicants who meet the essential criteria for the role, ensuring equitable opportunities for everyone.

If you require disability-related adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact DLO@cpfc.co.uk (DLO inbox is for disability-related queries only, if you wish to gain further information regarding this vacancy, please contact cpfc.recruitment@cpfc.co.uk). We are here to help you through the whole recruitment process including trying to apply for a vacancy, getting in touch with us through the process, and/or helping with access in case you are required to come in for an interview.


Safeguarding

Crystal Palace Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and vulnerable people and expects all staff to share this commitment. As such, the post holder will be required to undergo any relevant security checks, including a comprehensive background check, Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) to ensure the safety and protection of children and vulnerable individuals.

job.title: Matchday Public Catering Chef
job.location: Selhurst Park Stadium, London, London, SE25 6PU, GB
job.html_description:
Job Location:      Selhurst Park Stadium, SE25 6PU

Contract Type:   0-hour, Fixed-Term Contract

Who we are:

We are Crystal Palace Football Club, a Premier League Football Club with more than 160 years of proud history. Spread across three different sites - brand new main offices at Selhurst Park Stadium, our London office, and our Training Ground/Academy, we offer the best atmosphere and culture for our fans and our employees. 


We strive for excellence in the workplace with a philosophy of high standards, professionalism, and a strong work ethic from our employees. We are constantly trying to grow, diversify and build with not only our team on the pitch, but also our team in the offices and on the ground.

About this role:

If you have experience in preparing and cooking hot food or as a grill chef with a passion for customer service – we want to hear from you! You will be engaging, enthusiastic and have a thirst to learn and grow. You want to be a part of the buzz and excitement of a match day by being the face of Crystal Palace Football Club and serving high quality products throughout the match.

Our match days are typically on Saturdays with some weekday evening and Sunday matches and the typical shift day is 8 hours depending on the game.

Responsibilities:

  • Understanding all menus, product offerings, packaging and pricing

  • Responsible for preparing and cooking all the hot food such as burgers, pies, hot dogs nachos and specialty items for a unit on match day and meeting peak periods with appropriate food levels

  • Maintain high standards of food presentation and quality with regular controls

  • Ensure all products are accounted for, prepared and distributed according to cook-off numbers and instructions from your manager

  • Restock and ensure the food prep area is clean at the end of your shift

  • Ensure all kitchen unit equipment (electrical or otherwise) is cleaned down and properly maintained at the end of every shift

  • Adhere and keep up to date records for food and health and safety standards such as food temperature control levels and following the club’s Food Safety Management System

  • Adhering to all Health & Safety policies and Security policies

  • Carrying out any other reasonable duties asked of you from within your team and management including being flexible in covering tasks outside of your role-specific duties.

  • Other duties and responsibilities as required by line manager.

  • Upholding and promoting the Club’s policies and procedures, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Equal Opportunities policies and procedures.


Experience, Skills and Qualifications:

  • Food Safety Level 2 certificate and strong knowledge of proper food handling procedures

  • Experience grilling or cooking food in mass quantities, particularly in a busy or similar environment

  • A can-do attitude and be prepared to help the rest of the team out with a positive attitude

  • Immaculate grooming standards and overall appearance

  • Always look for ways to improve

  • Can work in high pressure situations

Our commitment to Equality

At Crystal Palace Football Club, Equality, Diversity & Inclusion is spirited and continues to grow due to the working environment, which makes all staff feel appreciated and welcomed. Crystal Palace Football Club do this by encouraging good relations and practices towards different groups and are committed to promoting employment opportunities that celebrate diversity.

We are committed to attracting, recruiting, and retaining the most talented candidates and the club intends to ensure that everyone who wishes to engage with the club, whether as matchday fans, staff, players, board members, participants in Foundation programmes and any other person engaged with the club's activities, has a real and equal opportunity to do so.


Reasonable Adjustment

We are proud to be a Disability Confident Employer. We guarantee an interview to all disabled applicants who meet the essential criteria for the role, ensuring equitable opportunities for everyone.

If you require disability-related adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact DLO@cpfc.co.uk (DLO inbox is for disability-related queries only, if you wish to gain further information regarding this vacancy, please contact cpfc.recruitment@cpfc.co.uk). We are here to help you through the whole recruitment process including trying to apply for a vacancy, getting in touch with us through the process, and/or helping with access in case you are required to come in for an interview.


Safeguarding

Crystal Palace Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and vulnerable people and expects all staff to share this commitment. As such, the post holder will be required to undergo any relevant security checks, including a comprehensive background check, Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) to ensure the safety and protection of children and vulnerable individuals.

job.title: Chief People Officer
job.location: Selhurst Park Stadium, London, London, SE25 6PU, GB
job.html_description:
Job Location:      Selhurst Park Stadium, SE25 6PU

Contract Type:   Full-time

Deadline:            15th March 2026

Who we are:

We are Crystal Palace Football Club, a Premier League Football Club with more than 160 years of proud history. Spread across three different sites - brand new main offices at Selhurst Park Stadium, our London office, and our Training Ground/Academy, we offer the best atmosphere and culture for our fans and our employees. 


We strive for excellence in the workplace with a philosophy of high standards, professionalism, and a strong work ethic from our employees. We are constantly trying to grow, diversify and build with not only our team on the pitch, but also our team in the offices and on the ground.

About this role:

Crystal Palace FC is looking for an operationally focused Chief People Officer (CPO) to lead and manage the Club’s people function, ensuring HR operations are delivered consistently, efficiently and to the highest standards. This is a senor leadership role responsible for overseeing all core people processes, maintaining compliance, and driving operational excellence across the full employee lifecycle.

In addition, the Chief People Officer will oversee the Club’s payroll and safeguarding functions. Act as the Club’s EDI and sustainability lead as well as the Club’s Senior Safeguarding Lead. The CPO will be responsible for ensuring all regulatory obligations are met including PL, FA and statutory requirements.

Responsibilities:


Leadership, Management & Governance

  • Provide leadership and guidance to the HR, Safeguarding and EDI team.

  • As part of the Senior Leadership Team the CPO will provide advice and guidance pertaining to ER, EDI, talent acquisition and L&D initiatives.

  • Be the subject matter expert to all managers and employees regarding people related matters

  • Design, implement and maintain the people strategy for the Club.

  • Ensure the tactical action plan and objectives reflect the overall people strategy

  • Constantly review the people strategy and operations to ensure the highest level of service is provided

  • Act as the EDI Lead for the Club

  • Ensure the Operational Board are fully aware and participate in the design, implementation and maintenance of the EDI strategy.

People Operations & Compliance

  • Oversee all people operations and be actively involved in all organisational change initiatives

  • Have a hands on approach to all people related operations

  • Act as the subject matter expert for employment law and employee relations issues

  • Overseeing talent acquisition, onboarding, talent management, upskilling, succession planning and performance management to ensure the right people with the right skills are in place.

  • Ensure all policies and procedures are reviewed on a regular basis and are up to date with changes in current legislation and Premier League/ FA requirements.

  • Ensure the Club are fulfilling its obligations with regards to the PLEDIS standard

  • Ensure the Club are fulfilling its obligations with regards to the FA rule N

  • Act as a champion for all EDI related activities and best practice.

Payroll, Reporting & Systems

  • Responsible for overseeing the payroll

  • Responsible for ensuring all statutory reports are submitted in accordance with designated deadlines

  • Ensure the HRIS used within the Club are fit for purpose

  • Provide high level KPI reports for senior management on a monthly basis

  • Provide ad hoc reports as and when requested

  • Analyse HR metrics and data in order to make informed decisions regarding HR processes and procedures

  • Liaise with finance to ensure all labour budgets are compiled at the start of each budget season

Safeguarding & Matchdays

  • Oversee the safeguarding function within the Club

  • Work alongside the Head of Safeguarding to ensure the safeguarding strategy is being executed in accordance with designated timelines.

  • Act as the Senior Safeguarding Lead for the Club and ensure all management are aware that safeguarding is a priority as part of everyday business.

  • Be present on matchdays to oversee the matchday staffing operation

  • Other duties and responsibilities as required by line manager.

  • Upholding and promoting the Club’s policies and procedures, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Equal Opportunities policies and procedures.


Experience, Skills and Qualifications:

  • A relevant degree level qualification

  • Chartered member of the CIPD

  • Demonstratable experience working in a senior people role within a fast-paced commercial environment.

  • Has an expert level of employment law and HR best practice knowledge

  • Experience working with different levels of management across multiple sites

  • Be commercially aware and have demonstrable experience in adding value to a business.

  • Excellent communication (including written, oral and presentation) and report writing

  • Personable, approachable and has excellent interpersonal skills

  • Discretion and an ability to keep sensitive information confidential

  • Ability to work under pressure and work to deadlines

  • Keen to continuously improve processes and systems

  • Experience working in the sporting industry (Desirable)

Benefits:

  • Complimentary match day ticket

  • Reward and Discount Scheme through our Tech Scheme and Simple Health app

  • Health and Wellbeing benefit scheme

  • 20% Discount in our Retail Stores

  • Discounts in various gyms through GymFlex

  • Volunteering Day - 1 Workday off to support a charity of your choice.

  • Travel Season Ticket loan

  • Holiday allowance: 25 Days 

Our commitment to Equality

At Crystal Palace Football Club, Equality, Diversity & Inclusion is spirited and continues to grow due to the working environment, which makes all staff feel appreciated and welcomed. Crystal Palace Football Club do this by encouraging good relations and practices towards different groups and are committed to promoting employment opportunities that celebrate diversity.

We are committed to attracting, recruiting, and retaining the most talented candidates and the club intends to ensure that everyone who wishes to engage with the club, whether as matchday fans, staff, players, board members, participants in Foundation programmes and any other person engaged with the club's activities, has a real and equal opportunity to do so.


Reasonable Adjustment

We are proud to be a Disability Confident Employer. We guarantee an interview to all disabled applicants who meet the essential criteria for the role, ensuring equitable opportunities for everyone.

If you require disability-related adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact DLO@cpfc.co.uk (DLO inbox is for disability-related queries only, if you wish to gain further information regarding this vacancy, please contact cpfc.recruitment@cpfc.co.uk). We are here to help you through the whole recruitment process including trying to apply for a vacancy, getting in touch with us through the process, and/or helping with access in case you are required to come in for an interview.


Safeguarding

Crystal Palace Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and vulnerable people and expects all staff to share this commitment. As such, the post holder will be required to undergo any relevant security checks, including a comprehensive background check, Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) to ensure the safety and protection of children and vulnerable individuals.
job.plain_text_description:
Job Location:Selhurst Park Stadium, SE25 6PU
Contract Type:Full-time
Deadline:15th March 2026
Who we are:
We are Crystal Palace Football Club, a Premier League Football Club with more than 160 years of proud history. Spread across three different sites - brand new main offices at Selhurst Park Stadium, our London office, and our Training Ground/Academy, we offer the best atmosphere and culture for our fans and our employees.
We strive for excellence in the workplace with a philosophy of high standards, professionalism, and a strong work ethic from our employees. We are constantly trying to grow, diversify and build with not only our team on the pitch, but also our team in the offices and on the ground.
About this role:
Crystal Palace FC is looking for an operationally focused Chief People Officer (CPO) to lead and manage the Club’s people function, ensuring HR operations are delivered consistently, efficiently and to the highest standards. This is a senor leadership role responsible for overseeing all core people processes, maintaining compliance, and driving operational excellence across the full employee lifecycle.
In addition, the Chief People Officer will oversee the Club’s payroll and safeguarding functions. Act as the Club’s EDI and sustainability lead as well as the Club’s Senior Safeguarding Lead. The CPO will be responsible for ensuring all regulatory obligations are met including PL, FA and statutory requirements.
Responsibilities:
Leadership, Management & Governance
- Provide leadership and guidance to the HR, Safeguarding and EDI team. - As part of the Senior Leadership Team the CPO will provide advice and guidance pertaining to ER, EDI, talent acquisition and L&D initiatives. - Be the subject matter expert to all managers and employees regarding people related matters - Design, implement and maintain the people strategy for the Club. - Ensure the tactical action plan and objectives reflect the overall people strategy - Constantly review the people strategy and operations to ensure the highest level of service is provided - Act as the EDI Lead for the Club - Ensure the Operational Board are fully aware and participate in the design, implementation and maintenance of the EDI strategy.
People Operations & Compliance
- Oversee all people operations and be actively involved in all organisational change initiatives - Have a hands on approach to all people related operations - Act as the subject matter expert for employment law and employee relations issues - Overseeing talent acquisition, onboarding, talent management, upskilling, succession planning and performance management to ensure the right people with the right skills are in place. - Ensure all policies and procedures are reviewed on a regular basis and are up to date with changes in current legislation and Premier League/ FA requirements. - Ensure the Club are fulfilling its obligations with regards to the PLEDIS standard - Ensure the Club are fulfilling its obligations with regards to the FA rule N - Act as a champion for all EDI related activities and best practice.
Payroll, Reporting & Systems
- Responsible for overseeing the payroll - Responsible for ensuring all statutory reports are submitted in accordance with designated deadlines - Ensure the HRIS used within the Club are fit for purpose - Provide high level KPI reports for senior management on a monthly basis - Provide ad hoc reports as and when requested - Analyse HR metrics and data in order to make informed decisions regarding HR processes and procedures - Liaise with finance to ensure all labour budgets are compiled at the start of each budget season
Safeguarding & Matchdays
- Oversee the safeguarding function within the Club - Work alongside the Head of Safeguarding to ensure the safeguarding strategy is being executed in accordance with designated timelines. - Act as the Senior Safeguarding Lead for the Club and ensure all management are aware that safeguarding is a priority as part of everyday business. - Be present on matchdays to oversee the matchday staffing operation
- Other duties and responsibilities as required by line manager. - Upholding and promoting the Club’s policies and procedures, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Equal Opportunities policies and procedures.
Experience, Skills and Qualifications:
- A relevant degree level qualification - Chartered member of the CIPD - Demonstratable experience working in a senior people role within a fast-paced commercial environment. - Has an expert level of employment law and HR best practice knowledge - Experience working with different levels of management across multiple sites - Be commercially aware and have demonstrable experience in adding value to a business. - Excellent communication (including written, oral and presentation) and report writing - Personable, approachable and has excellent interpersonal skills - Discretion and an ability to keep sensitive information confidential - Ability to work under pressure and work to deadlines - Keen to continuously improve processes and systems - Experience working in the sporting industry (Desirable)
Benefits:
- Complimentary match day ticket - Reward and Discount Scheme through our Tech Scheme and Simple Health app - Health and Wellbeing benefit scheme - 20% Discount in our Retail Stores - Discounts in various gyms through GymFlex - Volunteering Day - 1 Workday off to support a charity of your choice. - Travel Season Ticket loan - Holiday allowance: 25 Days
Our commitment to Equality
At Crystal Palace Football Club, Equality, Diversity & Inclusion is spirited and continues to grow due to the working environment, which makes all staff feel appreciated and welcomed. Crystal Palace Football Club do this by encouraging good relations and practices towards different groups and are committed to promoting employment opportunities that celebrate diversity.
We are committed to attracting, recruiting, and retaining the most talented candidates and the club intends to ensure that everyone who wishes to engage with the club, whether as matchday fans, staff, players, board members, participants in Foundation programmes and any other person engaged with the club's activities, has a real and equal opportunity to do so.
Reasonable Adjustment
We are proud to be a Disability Confident Employer. We guarantee an interview to all disabled applicants who meet the essential criteria for the role, ensuring equitable opportunities for everyone.
If you require disability-related adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact DLO@cpfc.co.uk (DLO inbox is for disability-related queries only, if you wish to gain further information regarding this vacancy, please contact cpfc.recruitment@cpfc.co.uk). We are here to help you through the whole recruitment process including trying to apply for a vacancy, getting in touch with us through the process, and/or helping with access in case you are required to come in for an interview.
Safeguarding
Crystal Palace Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and vulnerable people and expects all staff to share this commitment. As such, the post holder will be required to undergo any relevant security checks, including a comprehensive background check, Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) to ensure the safety and protection of children and vulnerable individuals.

job.title: First Team Soft Tissue Therapist (Casual/Part-time)
job.location: Copers Cope Road, Beckenham, London, BR3 1NZ, GB
job.html_description:
Job Location:     Academy Training Ground, BR3 1RJ

Contract Type:   Casual (0-hour contract)

Who we are:

We are Crystal Palace Football Club, a Premier League Football Club with more than 160 years of proud history. Spread across three different sites - brand new main offices at Selhurst Park Stadium, our London office, and our Training Ground/Academy, we offer the best atmosphere and culture for our fans and our employees. 


We strive for excellence in the workplace with a philosophy of high standards, professionalism, and a strong work ethic from our employees. We are constantly trying to grow, diversify and build with not only our team on the pitch, but also our team in the offices and on the ground.

About this role:

Crystal Palace FC is looking for outstanding Soft Tissue Therapists (part-time) to join our pool of casual staff providing soft tissues services to the First Team squad, and high-potential Academy players.

Responsibilities:

  • Deliver pre and post training soft tissue treatment to ensure optimum condition of players during daily training

  • Provide pre- and post-match soft-tissue therapy and recovery support for First Team players, including overnight stays at team hotels when required.

  • Assess and manage musculoskeletal problems which would be amenable to soft tissue therapy

  • Treat injured players in conjunction with the medical team to ensure optimum recovery and rehabilitation.

  • Work with the interdisciplinary team to individualise treatment and recovery strategies in accordance with the team periodisation.

  • Attend occasional home and away fixtures (including European travel) as required.

  • Maintain detailed and comprehensive clinical notes on the Smartabase electronic health records system

  • Communicate regularly with colleagues and key stakeholders to update on player status

  • Support the effective operation and maintenance of medical treatment environments across the training ground and stadium.

  • Participate in a programme of structured CPD specific to your individual needs, in the context of the wider departmental priorities.

  • Other duties and responsibilities as required by line manager.

  • Upholding and promoting the Club’s policies and procedures, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Equal Opportunities policies and procedures.


Experience, Skills and Qualifications:

Required

  • A minimum 2 years’ post-qualification experience with evidence of working in a professional sporting environment

  • Level 5 qualification in Sports Massage

  • Exceptional communication skills including the ability to communicate concisely, assertively, and effectively at all levels and across various professional disciplines

  • A track record in treating professional sports personnel to ensure the highest standard of service is provided to the players at all times.

  • Detailed awareness of Safeguarding procedures, and how to recognise and escalate concerns.

Desirable

  • BSc in a relevant subject

  • Qualifications and experience in the use of alternative soft tissue treatment modalities which can be appropriately, safely, and effectively used to treat professional footballers.

  • Advanced Trauma and Medical Management in Football (ATMMiF) qualification

  • FA Safeguarding Certification

  • Proficiency in an additional language (e.g., French, Spanish)

Our commitment to Equality

At Crystal Palace Football Club, Equality, Diversity & Inclusion is spirited and continues to grow due to the working environment, which makes all staff feel appreciated and welcomed. Crystal Palace Football Club do this by encouraging good relations and practices towards different groups and are committed to promoting employment opportunities that celebrate diversity.

We are committed to attracting, recruiting, and retaining the most talented candidates and the club intends to ensure that everyone who wishes to engage with the club, whether as matchday fans, staff, players, board members, participants in Foundation programmes and any other person engaged with the club's activities, has a real and equal opportunity to do so.


Reasonable Adjustment

We are proud to be a Disability Confident Employer. We guarantee an interview to all disabled applicants who meet the essential criteria for the role, ensuring equitable opportunities for everyone.

If you require disability-related adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact DLO@cpfc.co.uk (DLO inbox is for disability-related queries only, if you wish to gain further information regarding this vacancy, please contact cpfc.recruitment@cpfc.co.uk). We are here to help you through the whole recruitment process including trying to apply for a vacancy, getting in touch with us through the process, and/or helping with access in case you are required to come in for an interview.


Safeguarding

Crystal Palace Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and vulnerable people and expects all staff to share this commitment. As such, the post holder will be required to undergo any relevant security checks, including a comprehensive background check, Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) to ensure the safety and protection of children and vulnerable individuals.

job.title: Driver (Casual Contract)
job.location: Copers Cope Road, Beckenham, London, BR3 1NZ, GB
job.html_description:
Job Location:     Academy Training Ground, BR3 1NZ

Contract Type:   Casual 0-hour Contract

Pay rate: £13.15 per hour

Who we are:

We are Crystal Palace Football Club, a Premier League Football Club with more than 160 years of proud history. Spread across three different sites - brand new main offices at Selhurst Park Stadium, our London office, and our Training Ground/Academy, we offer the best atmosphere and culture for our fans and our employees. 


We strive for excellence in the workplace with a philosophy of high standards, professionalism, and a strong work ethic from our employees. We are constantly trying to grow, diversify and build with not only our team on the pitch, but also our team in the offices and on the ground.

About this role:

The Academy are looking for an experienced, friendly and reliable driver to provide transport support to Academy Players from U9 – U21. The successful candidate will transport Players to and from the Academy, to and from away fixtures and to ad-hoc appointments around the local area.

Responsibilities:

  • Provide transport to the Academy teams from U9-U21

  • Transport support to Academy players for Away fixtures and ad-hoc appointments

  • Other duties and responsibilities as required by line manager.

  • Upholding and promoting the Club’s policies and procedures, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Equal Opportunities policies and procedures.


Experience, Skills and Qualifications:

  • Clear full Driving Licence

  • Be over 21 years old

  • Good knowledge of London and surrounding areas

  • Experience working with young people

  • Flexible to work evening and weekends

  • High standards of punctuality, presentation and professionalism

job.title: Commercial Support Executive
job.location: Selhurst Park Stadium, London, London, SE25 6PU, GB
job.html_description:
Job Location:      Selhurst Park Stadium, SE25 6PU

Contract Type:   Full-time

Deadline:            18th March 2026

Who we are:

We are Crystal Palace Football Club, a Premier League Football Club with more than 160 years of proud history. Spread across three different sites - brand new main offices at Selhurst Park Stadium, our London office, and our Training Ground/Academy, we offer the best atmosphere and culture for our fans and our employees. 


We strive for excellence in the workplace with a philosophy of high standards, professionalism, and a strong work ethic from our employees. We are constantly trying to grow, diversify and build with not only our team on the pitch, but also our team in the offices and on the ground.

About this role

Our Premium matchday and non-matchday events business has recently delivered standout years and as a club we are now building on this success and looking to maintain momentum via ambitious plans for venue expansion.

We are looking for a dynamic individual who will play a pivotal role in helping to deliver exceptional non-matchday B2B and B2C events, as well ensuring seamless operations for our exclusive Premium matchday hospitality offerings. Ideal for an ambitious recent graduate or someone with real-world relevant experience looking for a foothold in a professional sports organisation. If you have a passion for sport, a keen eye for detail, and a drive to exceed expectations, we want you on our team.

Responsibilities:

  • Stadium Tours: Pre and post-tour administration, as well as, on the ground set-up of this hugely successful product, ensuring the venue is prepped and ready in advance of the official guides and guests arrivals

  • Event Planning Support: Assist in the planning and coordination of non-matchday events hosted by the club, including conferences, corporate functions, celebratory events, wakes, filming and production days, fan engagement events and more.

  • Commercial Team Event Delivery: Support the Commercial Team with administrative and operational requirements for team-led events such as Sporting Dinners, Business Clubs, Beer Festivals

  • Internal Bookings: Oversee and coordinate all internal bookings across the club and Palace for Life Foundation. Manage room allocations, confirm requirements, and ensure internal stakeholders are fully informed of processes, availability, and event logistics.

  • Logistics Coordination: Collaborate with relevant teams to manage event logistics, such as confirming venue bookings, overseeing equipment setup, coordinating catering arrangements, managing security considerations and more

  • On-Site Support: Assist in on-site event management, overseeing event operations, and troubleshooting any issues that may arise

  • Guest Relations: Provide exceptional customer service to event attendees, addressing queries, handling registrations, and ensuring a seamless customer experience

  • Premium Matchday Booking Management: Coordinate Premium matchday hospitality bookings with clients, ensuring accuracy of requirements and timely communication with them regarding ticketing, itineraries, menus, dietary requirements, and an ‘over and above’ level of customer service

  • Matchday Logistics: Collaborate with internal teams to ensure the smooth execution of Premium hospitality offerings along with clients’ varying requirements, as well as effectively raising client feedback during and post-match with the relevant personnel

  • Matchday Delivery: Support the Commercial Team to deliver an exceptional matchday experience to all guests, whether that be overseeing mascots, assisting at Main Reception or facilitating sponsor experiences

  • Other duties and responsibilities as required by line manager.

  • Upholding and promoting the Club’s policies and procedures, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Equal Opportunities policies and procedures.


Experience, Skills and Qualifications:

  • Confidence to interact, engage with and build professional relationships with a diverse range of matchday and non-matchday clients

  • An eye for identifying potential issues and trouble shooting

  • A collaborative spirit and the ability to work well within a diverse team environment

  • Strong organisational skills to handle and prioritise multiple tasks and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment

  • Flexibility to adapt to the dynamic nature of the sports industry and irregular working hours during matchdays and events.

  • Attention to detail and a can-do, problem-solving attitude and growth mindset

  • A good degree of business acumen and be able to spot commercial opportunities

  • The attitude of a self-starter who does not require micromanagement and can work independently

  • Proficiency in all Microsoft Office programs (Word, Outlook Excel etc.)

  • A degree in Event Management, Hospitality, Business Administration, or a related field) (desirable)

  • Excellent verbal and written communication

  • Or previous experience in event management, hospitality, or customer service roles (desirable)

  • Experience in working with Salesforce or similar CRM (desirable)

  • Experience in working with Ticketmaster (desirable)

Benefits:

  • Complimentary match day ticket

  • Reward and Discount Scheme through our Tech Scheme and Simple Health app

  • Health and Wellbeing benefit scheme

  • 20% Discount in our Retail Stores

  • Discounts in various gyms through GymFlex

  • Volunteering Day - 1 Workday off to support a charity of your choice.

  • Travel Season Ticket loan

  • Holiday allowance: 25 Days 

Our commitment to Equality

At Crystal Palace Football Club, Equality, Diversity & Inclusion is spirited and continues to grow due to the working environment, which makes all staff feel appreciated and welcomed. Crystal Palace Football Club do this by encouraging good relations and practices towards different groups and are committed to promoting employment opportunities that celebrate diversity.

We are committed to attracting, recruiting, and retaining the most talented candidates and the club intends to ensure that everyone who wishes to engage with the club, whether as matchday fans, staff, players, board members, participants in Foundation programmes and any other person engaged with the club's activities, has a real and equal opportunity to do so.


Reasonable Adjustment

We are proud to be a Disability Confident Employer. We guarantee an interview to all disabled applicants who meet the essential criteria for the role, ensuring equitable opportunities for everyone.

If you require disability-related adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact DLO@cpfc.co.uk (DLO inbox is for disability-related queries only, if you wish to gain further information regarding this vacancy, please contact cpfc.recruitment@cpfc.co.uk). We are here to help you through the whole recruitment process including trying to apply for a vacancy, getting in touch with us through the process, and/or helping with access in case you are required to come in for an interview.


Safeguarding

Crystal Palace Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and vulnerable people and expects all staff to share this commitment. As such, the post holder will be required to undergo any relevant security checks, including a comprehensive background check, Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) to ensure the safety and protection of children and vulnerable individuals.

Responsibilities:
- Stadium Tours: Pre and post-tour administration, as well as, on the ground set-up of this hugely successful product, ensuring the venue is prepped and ready in advance of the official guides and guests arrivals - Event Planning Support: Assist in the planning and coordination of non-matchday events hosted by the club, including conferences, corporate functions, celebratory events, wakes, filming and production days, fan engagement events and more. - Commercial Team Event Delivery: Support the Commercial Team with administrative and operational requirements for team-led events such as Sporting Dinners, Business Clubs, Beer Festivals - Internal Bookings: Oversee and coordinate all internal bookings across the club and Palace for Life Foundation. Manage room allocations, confirm requirements, and ensure internal stakeholders are fully informed of processes, availability, and event logistics. - Logistics Coordination: Collaborate with relevant teams to manage event logistics, such as confirming venue bookings, overseeing equipment setup, coordinating catering arrangements, managing security considerations and more - On-Site Support: Assist in on-site event management, overseeing event operations, and troubleshooting any issues that may arise - Guest Relations: Provide exceptional customer service to event attendees, addressing queries, handling registrations, and ensuring a seamless customer experience - Premium Matchday Booking Management: Coordinate Premium matchday hospitality bookings with clients, ensuring accuracy of requirements and timely communication with them regarding ticketing, itineraries, menus, dietary requirements, and an ‘over and above’ level of customer service - Matchday Logistics: Collaborate with internal teams to ensure the smooth execution of Premium hospitality offerings along with clients’ varying requirements, as well as effectively raising client feedback during and post-match with the relevant personnel - Matchday Delivery: Support the Commercial Team to deliver an exceptional matchday experience to all guests, whether that be overseeing mascots, assisting at Main Reception or facilitating sponsor experiences - Other duties and responsibilities as required by line manager. - Upholding and promoting the Club’s policies and procedures, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Equal Opportunities policies and procedures.
Experience, Skills and Qualifications:
- Confidence to interact, engage with and build professional relationships with a diverse range of matchday and non-matchday clients - An eye for identifying potential issues and trouble shooting - A collaborative spirit and the ability to work well within a diverse team environment - Strong organisational skills to handle and prioritise multiple tasks and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment - Flexibility to adapt to the dynamic nature of the sports industry and irregular working hours during matchdays and events. - Attention to detail and a can-do, problem-solving attitude and growth mindset - A good degree of business acumen and be able to spot commercial opportunities - The attitude of a self-starter who does not require micromanagement and can work independently - Proficiency in all Microsoft Office programs (Word, Outlook Excel etc.) - A degree in Event Management, Hospitality, Business Administration, or a related field) (desirable) - Excellent verbal and written communication - Or previous experience in event management, hospitality, or customer service roles (desirable) - Experience in working with Salesforce or similar CRM (desirable) - Experience in working with Ticketmaster (desirable)
Benefits:
- Complimentary match day ticket - Reward and Discount Scheme through our Tech Scheme and Simple Health app - Health and Wellbeing benefit scheme - 20% Discount in our Retail Stores - Discounts in various gyms through GymFlex - Volunteering Day - 1 Workday off to support a charity of your choice. - Travel Season Ticket loan - Holiday allowance: 25 Days
Our commitment to Equality
At Crystal Palace Football Club, Equality, Diversity & Inclusion is spirited and continues to grow due to the working environment, which makes all staff feel appreciated and welcomed. Crystal Palace Football Club do this by encouraging good relations and practices towards different groups and are committed to promoting employment opportunities that celebrate diversity.
We are committed to attracting, recruiting, and retaining the most talented candidates and the club intends to ensure that everyone who wishes to engage with the club, whether as matchday fans, staff, players, board members, participants in Foundation programmes and any other person engaged with the club's activities, has a real and equal opportunity to do so.
Reasonable Adjustment
We are proud to be a Disability Confident Employer. We guarantee an interview to all disabled applicants who meet the essential criteria for the role, ensuring equitable opportunities for everyone.
If you require disability-related adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact DLO@cpfc.co.uk (DLO inbox is for disability-related queries only, if you wish to gain further information regarding this vacancy, please contact cpfc.recruitment@cpfc.co.uk). We are here to help you through the whole recruitment process including trying to apply for a vacancy, getting in touch with us through the process, and/or helping with access in case you are required to come in for an interview.
Safeguarding
Crystal Palace Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and vulnerable people and expects all staff to share this commitment. As such, the post holder will be required to undergo any relevant security checks, including a comprehensive background check, Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) to ensure the safety and protection of children and vulnerable individuals.

job.title: Head of Education
job.location: Copers Cope Road, Beckenham, London, BR3 1NZ, GB
job.html_description:
Job Location:      Academy Training Ground, BR3 1NZ

Contract Type:   Full-time

Deadline:            26th March 2026

Who we are:

We are Crystal Palace Football Club, a Premier League Football Club with more than 160 years of proud history. Spread across three different sites - brand new main offices at Selhurst Park Stadium, our London office, and our Training Ground/Academy, we offer the best atmosphere and culture for our fans and our employees. 


We strive for excellence in the workplace with a philosophy of high standards, professionalism, and a strong work ethic from our employees. We are constantly trying to grow, diversify and build with not only our team on the pitch, but also our team in the offices and on the ground.

About this role:

To be responsible and progress all educational matters and welfare of all scholars and players at Crystal Palace Football Club Academy by managing the learning programme in terms of academic work and liaising with internal and external agencies such as Premier League, PFA and supporting academic bodies.

Responsibilities:

  • Monitor the educational progression of all Academy Players registered with the Club. This will be in accordance with the PL.

  • Attend at all formal parent consultations.

  • Ensure that the Academy’s educational provision reflects the strategy and performance targets identified in the Club’s Academy Performance Plan.

  • Work with the Academy Manager to ensure the Education Programme offers the best educational opportunities maximise the educational potential of young players.

  • Manage and safely store all data related to the Educational progress of young players in line with Data Protection Legislation.

  • Liaise and monitor the performance of all Academy players. Review educational progress and feedback to parents/carers.

  • To liaise with any partner agency involved in the Education of players

  • Manage and organise the UEFA C coaching certificate with the PFA

  • Deliver the Induction to scholars

  • To facilitate the education programme across the phases

  • Provide written reports on educational progress of players

  • Support the release of any player from the Academy alongside the recruitment department

  • Attend all workshops by the PL or other agencies associated with the football club

  • To manage any budget related to the post

  • Provide CPD events for staff

  • Hold inter-departmental meetings

  • To manage a lifestyle programme of activities for young players outside of football

  • To oversee and develop the Hybrid Programme for the U13 – U16 schoolboys including liaising with parents, carers and schools.

  • Work directly with the Player Liaison and support with host families and trialists

  • Work closely with the Safeguarding Manager on any issues relating to the Academy

  • Create a ‘Career Fair’ for U16-U19s to be held in October/November each year.

  • Other duties and responsibilities as required by line manager.

  • Upholding and promoting the Club’s policies and procedures, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Equal Opportunities policies and procedures.


Experience, Skills and Qualifications:

  • Qualified Teacher Status (QTS)

  • Secondary school teaching experience

  • Current FA Safeguarding Certificate

  • Full clean UK Driving License

  • Experience working in a similar role in professional sport or Elite environment (desirable)

Benefits:

  • Complimentary match day ticket

  • Reward and Discount Scheme through our Tech Scheme and Simple Health app

  • Health and Wellbeing benefit scheme

  • 20% Discount in our Retail Stores

  • Discounts in various gyms through GymFlex

  • Volunteering Day - 1 Workday off to support a charity of your choice.

  • Travel Season Ticket loan

  • Holiday allowance: 25 Days 

Our commitment to Equality

At Crystal Palace Football Club, Equality, Diversity & Inclusion is spirited and continues to grow due to the working environment, which makes all staff feel appreciated and welcomed. Crystal Palace Football Club do this by encouraging good relations and practices towards different groups and are committed to promoting employment opportunities that celebrate diversity.

We are committed to attracting, recruiting, and retaining the most talented candidates and the club intends to ensure that everyone who wishes to engage with the club, whether as matchday fans, staff, players, board members, participants in Foundation programmes and any other person engaged with the club's activities, has a real and equal opportunity to do so.


Reasonable Adjustment

We are proud to be a Disability Confident Employer. We guarantee an interview to all disabled applicants who meet the essential criteria for the role, ensuring equitable opportunities for everyone.

If you require disability-related adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact DLO@cpfc.co.uk (DLO inbox is for disability-related queries only, if you wish to gain further information regarding this vacancy, please contact cpfc.recruitment@cpfc.co.uk). We are here to help you through the whole recruitment process including trying to apply for a vacancy, getting in touch with us through the process, and/or helping with access in case you are required to come in for an interview.


Safeguarding

Crystal Palace Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and vulnerable people and expects all staff to share this commitment. As such, the post holder will be required to undergo any relevant security checks, including a comprehensive background check, Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) to ensure the safety and protection of children and vulnerable individuals.

job.title: Chief of Staff
job.location: Selhurst Park Stadium, London, London, SE25 6PU, GB
job.html_description:
Job Location:      Selhurst Park Stadium, SE25 6PU

Contract Type:   Full-time

Deadline:            26th Match 2026

Who we are:

We are Crystal Palace Football Club, a Premier League Football Club with more than 160 years of proud history. Spread across three different sites - brand new main offices at Selhurst Park Stadium, our London office, and our Training Ground/Academy, we offer the best atmosphere and culture for our fans and our employees. 


We strive for excellence in the workplace with a philosophy of high standards, professionalism, and a strong work ethic from our employees. We are constantly trying to grow, diversify and build with not only our team on the pitch, but also our team in the offices and on the ground.

About this role:

The Chief of Staff serves as a strategic partner to the Chairman, providing high-level analytical and reporting support to inform executive decision-making. This role is responsible for preparing weekly shareholder reports, conducting business performance analysis, and identifying key trends across the organization. The Chief of Staff translates complex business data into clear, executive-level PowerPoint presentations and actionable insights for leadership and stakeholders.

This position does not have direct operational oversight but focuses on business analytics, trend evaluation, and performance reporting to support strategic planning and communication at the board and shareholder level

Responsibilities:

Strategic Leadership

  • Act as a trusted advisor to the Chairman

  • Lead on high-impact strategic initiatives

  • Present financial and strategic insights with clarity, precision and confidence

  • Translate complex financial and operational data into clear insights and actionable recommendations

Market Trends & Competitive Intelligence

  • Build and maintain frameworks to monitor industry dynamics especially in relation commercial and financial trends

  • Analyse market trends, competitive positioning, and macroeconomic factors to inform strategic decisions.

  • Advise the Chairman on strategic implications of market changes and help inform investment, pricing, expansion and other business related decisions

Stakeholder Engagement

  • Develop highly detailed, logically structured, and visually compelling presentations for Board and investor audiences

  • Prepare briefing notes and analytical summaries for board and external stakeholder meetings

  • Project & Program Management

  • Support capital allocation decisions, investment appraisals, and growth strategy initiatives

  • Oversee priority projects, ensuring delivery is on time and within budget.

  • Support budget planning processes

  • Set frameworks for performance tracking, reporting, and continuous improvement

  • Other duties and responsibilities as required by line manager.

  • Upholding and promoting the Club’s policies and procedures, including Health and Safety, Safeguarding and Equal Opportunities policies and procedures.


Experience, Skills and Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Mathematics, or related field (essential)

  • Post graduate degree preferred (MBA, Msc or equivalent)

  • Background in investment banking, private equity, strategy consulting, or a similarly rigorous analytical environment preferred.

  • Strong understanding of market dynamics and financial reporting

  • Exceptional business acumen, financial knowledge and analytical capabilities as well as a high level of quantitative ability

  • Strong written and verbal communication skills; able to influence stakeholders across all levels and ability to simplify complex financial concepts

  • Strong organisational, time-management and prioritisation abilities

  • Highly analytical, logical thinker with structured problem – solving skills

  • Impeccable attention to detail and accuracy and

  • Commercially minded with strategic depth

  • Highly proficient with data analysis tools, market research and investment platforms

  • Outstanding presentation building capability with the ability to create well structured and easily digestible executive-level slides.

  • Advanced proficiency in financial modelling and valuation methodologies

  • Intellectually curious who can remain calm under pressure and thrives in high – performance environments.

  • Discreet, trustworthy and capable of operating at board level

  • High intellectual horsepower; able to process and synthesize large volumes of information rapidly

  • Proactive, self-directed, and execution- focused

Benefits:

  • Complimentary match day ticket

  • Reward and Discount Scheme through our Tech Scheme and Simple Health app

  • Health and Wellbeing benefit scheme

  • 20% Discount in our Retail Stores

  • Discounts in various gyms through GymFlex

  • Volunteering Day - 1 Workday off to support a charity of your choice.

  • Travel Season Ticket loan

  • Holiday allowance: 25 Days 

Our commitment to Equality

At Crystal Palace Football Club, Equality, Diversity & Inclusion is spirited and continues to grow due to the working environment, which makes all staff feel appreciated and welcomed. Crystal Palace Football Club do this by encouraging good relations and practices towards different groups and are committed to promoting employment opportunities that celebrate diversity.

We are committed to attracting, recruiting, and retaining the most talented candidates and the club intends to ensure that everyone who wishes to engage with the club, whether as matchday fans, staff, players, board members, participants in Foundation programmes and any other person engaged with the club's activities, has a real and equal opportunity to do so.


Reasonable Adjustment

We are proud to be a Disability Confident Employer. We guarantee an interview to all disabled applicants who meet the essential criteria for the role, ensuring equitable opportunities for everyone.

If you require disability-related adjustments during the recruitment process, please contact DLO@cpfc.co.uk (DLO inbox is for disability-related queries only, if you wish to gain further information regarding this vacancy, please contact cpfc.recruitment@cpfc.co.uk). We are here to help you through the whole recruitment process including trying to apply for a vacancy, getting in touch with us through the process, and/or helping with access in case you are required to come in for an interview.


Safeguarding

Crystal Palace Football Club is committed to safeguarding and ensuring the welfare and protection of children and vulnerable people and expects all staff to share this commitment. As such, the post holder will be required to undergo any relevant security checks, including a comprehensive background check, Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) to ensure the safety and protection of children and vulnerable individuals.

job.title: Sicherheitsmitarbeiter (m/w/d) Objektschutz
job.html_description:
Wir sind Borussia Dortmund. Nicht nur ein weltweit bekannter Fußballverein, sondern auch ein börsennotiertes Unternehmen mit über 900 Mitarbeiter*innen. Neben unserem Kerngeschäft des Fußballs sind wir in zahlreichen weiteren Geschäftsfeldern tätig. Die Borussia Dortmund GmbH & Co. KGaA fungiert als Dachgesellschaft für neun Tochterunternehmen.

Für die BVB Stadionmanagement GmbH suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt einen Sicherheitsmitarbeiter (m/w/d) im Objektschutz in Vollzeit (174h/Monat).

Deine Aufgaben
  • Objektschutztätigkeiten im SIGNAL IDUNA PARK
  • Pfortendienste sowie Kontroll- und Schließrunden
  • Empfang, Anmeldung und Betreuung von Besuchern, Lieferanten und Dienstleistern
  • Zutrittskontrolle zur Liegenschaft (Personen-, Fahrzeug- und Taschenkontrolle)
  • Bedienung der Sicherheitstechnik
  • Dokumentation von Vorkommnissen
Dein Profil
  • Mindestens Unterrichtungsnachweis der IHK gem. § 34a GewO
  • IHK Sachkundeprüfung gem. § 34a GewO wünschenswert
  • Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im Objektschutz 
  • Technisches Grundverständnis, z. B. Bedienung von Videotechnik
  • Ausgeprägte Kommunikations- und Teamfähigkeit
  • Souveränes Auftreten und selbstständiges Arbeiten
  • Sicherer Umgang mit modernen Kommunikationsmitteln und den gängigen MS Office-Anwendungen
  • Bereitschaft zum Schichtdienst auch an Wochenenden und Feiertagen
  • Gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
Wir bieten
  • Ein Platz in einem dynamischen Team, das sich stark mit seinen Aufgaben identifiziert
  • Spannende und vielseitige Tätigkeiten in einem einzigartigen Arbeitsumfeld
  • Kurze Entscheidungswege und viel eigenverantwortlicher Gestaltungsspielraum
  • Ein faires monatliches Festgehalt
  • Persönliches Mitarbeiter-Ticket für unsere Heimspiele
  • Individuelle Maßnahmen zur beruflichen und persönlichen Weiterentwicklung
  • Kostenfreie Mitgliedschaft im Fitnessstudio sowie Unterstützung für einen gesunden Arbeitsalltag
  • Weitere Mehrwerte, wie z. B. Dienstrad-Leasing, Team-Events, Mitarbeitenden-Bistro, Altersvorsorge etc.
Jetzt bewerben

Dann freuen wir uns auf Deine aussagekräftige Bewerbung mit Anschreiben, Lebenslauf, Arbeitszeugnissen und dem Nachweis für die Unterrichtung bzw. Sachkundeprüfung der IHK gem. § 34a GewO.

Bitte bewirb Dich online über den "Jetzt-bewerben Button".

BVB Stadionmanagement GmbH

job.title: Greenkeeper (m/w/d)
job.html_description:
Wir sind Borussia Dortmund. Nicht nur ein weltweit bekannter Fußballverein, sondern auch ein börsennotiertes Unternehmen mit über 900 Mitarbeiter*innen. Neben unserem Kerngeschäft des Fußballs sind wir in zahlreichen weiteren Geschäftsfeldern tätig. Die Borussia Dortmund GmbH & Co. KGaA fungiert als Dachgesellschaft für neun Tochterunternehmen.

Zur Unterstützung unseres Rasenteams in der BVB Stadionmanagement GmbH suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt einen Greenkeeper (m/w/d) im Rahmen einer Vollzeitbeschäftigung (42 Std./Woche).

Deine Aufgaben
  • Professionelle Pflege und Unterhaltung der Sportrasenflächen und Grünanlagen, z. B. Mähen, Düngen, Bewässern, Nachsäen, Vertikutieren, Aerifizieren, Besanden etc.
  • Beseitigung von Unkräutern, Laub, Grün- und Grasschnitt, Unrat, Abfällen usw.
  • Bedienung und Führung von Arbeitsmaschinen und -geräten sowie deren Pflege und Instandhaltung
  • Vorbereitung und Pflege der Spielfelder und Nebenflächen für den Trainings- und Spielbetrieb im SIGNAL IDUNA PARK und am Trainingsgelände Brackel
  • Auf- und Abbau sowie Kontrolle von baulichen und technischen Anlagen, z. B. Tore, Ballfangnetze, Eckfahnen etc.
  • Spielfeldbetreuung an Heimspieltagen der Profi- und Nachwuchsmannschaften
Dein Profil
  • Abgeschlossene handwerkliche Ausbildung, idealerweise im Garten- bzw. Landschaftsbau oder einschlägige Berufserfahrung in der Rasenpflege
  • Handwerkliches Geschick sowie ein gutes Gespür für die Maschinenführung
  • Selbstständige, strukturierte und proaktive Arbeitsweise
  • Spaß an der Zusammenarbeit im Team
  • Hohes Maß an Zuverlässigkeit und Verantwortungsbewusstsein
  • Hohe Einsatzbereitschaft und Flexibilität sowie Bereitschaft zu Arbeitszeiten an Spieltagen, Wochenenden und am Abend
  • Führerschein Klasse B, Klasse BE wünschenswert
Das erwartet Dich
  • Ein Platz in einem dynamischen Team, das sich stark mit seinen Aufgaben identifiziert
  • Spannende und vielseitige Tätigkeiten in einem einzigartigen Arbeitsumfeld
  • Kurze Entscheidungswege und viel eigenverantwortlicher Gestaltungsspielraum
  • Persönliches Mitarbeiter-Ticket für unsere Heimspiele
  • Individuelle Maßnahmen zur beruflichen und persönlichen Weiterentwicklung
  • Kostenfreie Mitgliedschaft im Fitnessstudio sowie Unterstützung für einen gesunden Arbeitsalltag
  • Weitere Mehrwerte, wie z. B. flexible Arbeitszeiten, Dienstrad-Leasing, Team-Events, Mitarbeiter-Bistro, Altersvorsorge etc.
Jetzt bewerben

Dann freuen wir uns auf Deine aussagekräftige Bewerbung mit Anschreiben, Lebenslauf und Zeugnissen. Bitte bewirb Dich online über den "Jetzt-bewerben Button".

job.title: Ordner (m/w/d) im BVB Ordnungsdienst
job.html_description:
Wir sind Borussia Dortmund. Nicht nur ein weltweit bekannter Fußballverein, sondern auch ein börsennotiertes Unternehmen mit über 900 Mitarbeiter*innen. Neben unserem Kerngeschäft des Fußballs sind wir in zahlreichen weiteren Geschäftsfeldern tätig. Die Borussia Dortmund GmbH & Co. KGaA fungiert als Dachgesellschaft für acht Tochterunternehmen.

Zur Gewährleistung eines reibungslosen Veranstaltungsverlaufes betreibt der BVB seit mehr als 30 Jahren einen vereinseigenen Ordnungsdienst für die Heimspiele im SIGNAL IDUNA PARK.

Für die aktuelle Saison suchen wir Verstärkung für unseren vereinseigenen Ordnungsdienst im Rahmen einer Nebentätigkeit.

Deine Aufgaben
  • Ticket- und Personenkontrollen
  • Freihalten von Rettungswegen
  • Platzanweisung
  • Spielfeldabsicherung
  • Sicherstellung der Einhaltung der Stadionordnung
Dein Profil
  • Mindestens 18 Jahre alt
  • Eintragsfreies Führungszeugnis
  • Zuverlässigkeit, Freundlichkeit, Teamfähigkeit
  • Körperliche Belastbarkeit und souveränen Umgang mit Stresssituationen
  • Gute Deutschkenntnisse
  • Bereitschaft zur Arbeit am Abend, an Wochenenden sowie an Feiertagen
Das erwartet Dich
  • Eine Ausbildung gem. DFB-Richtlinien im Bereich QuaSOD (Qualifizierung Sicherheits- und Ordnungsdienste)
  • Arbeiten in einem hoch emotionalen und spannenden Umfeld
  • Einen Platz in einer dynamischen Mannschaft
  • Eine faire Vergütung
  • Einsätze bei den Heimspielen der Profimannschaft, der U23, den BVB-Frauen und bei Sonderveranstaltungen
Jetzt bewerben

Wir freuen uns auf Deine aussagekräftige Bewerbung. Bitte bewirb Dich online über den "Jetzt-bewerben-Button".

job.title: Product Owner (m/w/d) Azure Middleware
job.html_description:
Wir sind Borussia Dortmund. Nicht nur ein weltweit bekannter Fußballverein, sondern auch ein börsennotiertes Unternehmen mit über 900 Mitarbeiter*innen. Neben unserem Kerngeschäft des Fußballs sind wir in zahlreichen weiteren Geschäftsfeldern tätig. Die Borussia Dortmund GmbH & Co. KGaA fungiert als Dachgesellschaft für sieben Tochterunternehmen.

In unserer IT-Abteilung dreht sich alles um digitale Technologien und innovative Lösungen. Unser Team sorgt nicht nur für die reibungslose Betreuung aller BVB-Webauftritte, sondern unterstützt auch unsere Merchandising-Prozesse und verwaltet technische Schnittstellen zwischen verschiedensten Systemen. Wir gewährleisten höchste Sicherheitsstandards für unsere digitale Infrastruktur und kümmern uns um die optimale Einrichtung von Arbeitsplätzen. Außerdem sind wir für alle EDV-technischen Belange rund um das Stadion verantwortlich.

Für die Verstärkung des Teams Softwareentwicklung in der Abteilung IT der Borussia Dortmund GmbH & Co. KGaA suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt einen Product Owner (m/w/d) Azure Middleware in Vollzeitanstellung (40h/Woche).

Deine Aufgaben
  • Verantwortung für Betrieb, Stabilität und Performance der Microsoft Azure-basierten Middleware-Plattform
  • Planung, Steuerung und Priorisierung von Weiterentwicklungen der Middleware unter Berücksichtigung technischer und fachlicher Anforderungen
  • Koordination und Abstimmung mit internen und externen Entwicklungspartnern sowie relevanten Schnittstellen
  • Sicherstellung der Einhaltung von Qualitätsstandards, IT-Sicherheitsrichtlinien und regulatorischen Anforderungen
  • Durchführung von Anforderungsanalysen, Aufwandsschätzungen sowie fachlicher Bewertung von Lösungsansätzen
  • Moderation von Abstimmungsrunden mit Stakeholdern sowie die transparente Kommunikation von Fortschritten, Risiken und Abhängigkeiten
  • Aufbau und Pflege einer belastbaren und realistischen Produkt-Roadmap
  • Unterstützung beim Incident-, Change- und Problem-Management im Zusammenhang mit der Middleware
Dein Profil
  • Abgeschlossenes Studium der (Wirtschafts-) Informatik, oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation
  • Fundierte Erfahrung im Betrieb und in der Weiterentwicklung komplexer Middleware-Lösungen, idealerweise in Microsoft Azure 
  • Erfahrung in der Rolle als Produktverantwortlicher oder technischer Projektleiter*in mit Blick auf Stakeholdermanagement
  • Souveränität im Umgang mit unterschiedlichen Anforderungen und Prioritäten aus Fachbereichen
  • Ausgeprägte analytische und konzeptionelle Fähigkeiten sowie eine strukturierte, lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise
  • Erfahrung im agilen Arbeiten (Scrum, Kanban) sowie im Umgang mit DevOps- und CI/CD-Prinzipien von Vorteil
  • Hohes Maß an Serviceorientierung, Qualitätsbewusstsein und Überzeugungskraft
  • Hohe Einsatzbereitschaft, Ambition und Leidenschaft
  • Kommunikationsstärke und Spaß an der Zusammenarbeit im Team
  • Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
Das erwartet Dich
  • Eine sich stetig weiterentwickelnde IT-Landschaft mit Möglichkeiten zur Mitgestaltung
  • Einen Platz in einer dynamischen Mannschaft, die eine hohe Identifikation mit ihren Aufgaben mitbringt
  • Spannende und vielseitige Tätigkeiten in einem einzigartigen Arbeitsumfeld
  • Kurze Entscheidungswege und viel eigenverantwortlicher Gestaltungsspielraum
  • Persönliches Mitarbeiter-Ticket für unsere Heimspiele
  • Individuelle Maßnahmen zur beruflichen und persönlichen Weiterentwicklung
  • Kostenfreie Mitgliedschaft im Fitnessstudio sowie Unterstützung für einen gesunden Arbeitsalltag
  • Weitere Mehrwerte, wie z.B. flexible Arbeitszeiten, Dienstrad-Leasing, Team-Events, Mitarbeiter-Kantine, Altersvorsorge etc.
Jetzt bewerben

Wir freuen uns auf Deine aussagekräftige Bewerbung mit Motivationsschreiben, Lebenslauf und Zeugnissen. Bitte bewirb Dich online über den "Jetzt-bewerben Button".

job.title: Aushilfe Public Catering
job.html_description:
Wir sind Borussia Dortmund. Nicht nur ein weltweit bekannter Fußballverein, sondern auch ein börsennotiertes Unternehmen mit über 990 Mitarbeiter*innen. Neben unserem Kerngeschäft des Fußballs sind wir in zahlreichen weiteren Geschäftsfeldern tätig. Die Borussia Dortmund GmbH & Co. KGaA fungiert als Dachgesellschaft für sieben Tochterunternehmen.

Die BVB Event & Catering GmbH ist für die Vermarktung des SIGNAL IDUNA PARK als einzigartige Eventlocation verantwortlich und bewirtschaftet die gastronomischen Flächen des Stadions an Spieltagen sowie bei zahlreichen weiteren Events. Dazu zählen neben Firmen-, Incentive- und Privatveranstaltungen aller Art auch Tagungen, Konferenzen und Messen, die auf allen Tribünen und Ebenen im Stadion stattfinden.

Für das Team Public Catering suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt Unterstützung im Rahmen einer Aushilfsbeschäftigung.

Deine Aufgaben
  • Organisation und Betreuung der Catering-Stände sowie Koordination des Standpersonals im SIGNAL IDUNA PARK an Heimspieltagen
  • Sicherstellung der effizienten Versorgung sowie Durchführung regelmäßiger Qualitätskontrollen an den Catering-Ständen
  • Unterstützung des Public Catering Teams bei der Vor- und Nachbereitung der Spieltage
Dein Profil
  • Verfügbarkeit an Heimspieltagen
  • Hohe Einsatzbereitschaft, Eigeninitiative und Lösungsorientierung
  • Spaß an der Zusammenarbeit im Team
  • Ausgeprägte Kontaktstärke sowie ein souveränes Auftreten
  • Hohe Stressresistenz und diplomatisches Verhalten auch in schwierigen Situationen
Das erwartet Dich
  • Arbeiten in einem hochemotionalen und spannenden Umfeld
  • Die Möglichkeit, Bundesliga-Luft zu schnuppern
  • Arbeitszeiten, die sich an Spieltagen orientieren
  • Einen Platz in einer dynamischen Mannschaft, die eine hohe Identifikation mit ihren Aufgaben mitbringt
Jetzt bewerben

Dann freuen wir uns auf Deine aussagekräftige Bewerbung mit Motivationsschreiben, Lebenslauf und Zeugnissen. Bitte bewirb Dich online über den "Jetzt-bewerben Button".
