job.plain_text_description:
In-House Employment Lawyer
We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our Liverpool FC, in-house Legal team. We’re looking for an individual with expert commercial employment lawyer to provide pragmatic, timely advice across the Club, helping to ensure our people practices, decision making and policies comply with employment legislation, data protection requirements and international employment standards.
In this high volume, fast paced environment, you’ll report to the Vice President, Legal and partner closely with the People & Culture team, offering hands on case management., and proactive legal insight to minimise risk and strengthen internal capability.
You will be expected to handle complex ER casework, DSARs, contractual queries and global employment issues.
What will you be doing?
Employment Law Advise
- Provide expert, commercially focused employment law advice to leaders and People & Culture teams and business leaders.. - Advise across all areas of UK employment law, - Monitor developments in employment legislation and ensure the Club remains compliant and future‑ready. - Produce clear legal guidance, risk assessments and recommended options.
Complex Case Management
- Lead ER cases - Guide the ER Manager and People team - Coach managers and People Partners on case strategy and risk management.
Data Protection & DSARs
- Lead all employment‑related DSAR responses in partnership with People Services and Data Protection teams. - Advise on GDPR compliance in investigations, suspensions and information sharing. - Recommend process improvements to reduce risk and improve efficiency.
Contracts, Policies & Governance
- Draft and review employment contracts, senior agreements, and people policies. - Ensure policies are legally compliant and aligned with Club culture. - Support global/overseas contract implementation and compliance.
Global & Overseas Employment Support
- Provide employment law advice for international hires, assignments and cross‑border working. - Ensure alignment with local labour laws and global mobility requirements.
Stakeholder Management & Collaboration
- Build strong partnerships across Legal, People & Culture, Safeguarding, Finance and operational teams. - Manage relationships with external legal advisers to ensure quality and cost control.
Capability Building & Continuous Improvement
- Deliver training on employment law, ER processes, DSARs and governance. - Embed strong legal reasoning across people processes to reduce risk. - Promote consistent, transparent and fair decision‑making across the Club
Who are we looking for?
- Able to translate complex legal principles into clear, practical, and commercially focused advice. - Handle sensitive information with discretion, sound judgment, and absolute integrity. - Excellent written and verbal communication skills. - Confident presenting to senior stakeholders. - Strong analytical ability and able to work accurately under pressure. - Highly organised, with the ability to manage a high volume of varied work. - Collaborative, people‑focused and solutions‑oriented approach. - Acts as a role model for the Club’s values, demonstrating professionalism, trust and accountability in all situations. - Calm, pragmatic, and with strong attention detail. - Committed to continuous improvement and developing capability across the organisation. - Qualified solicitor (England & Wales) with minimum 5 years’ PQE in employment law. - Current practicing certificate (SRA) with up-to-date CPD - Strong background in high risk, complex employee relations and Employment Tribunal litigation. - Experience working in fast paced, high profile or highly regulated environments (e.g. sport, media, large corporates). - Demonstrable experience handling DSARs, GDPR related issues and data heavy case management. - Confident in drafting and negotiating employment contracts, policies, settlement agreements and international terms.
Desirable Experience
- Experience advising on overseas employment matters or global mobility - Experience of working in house - Experience of commercial legal work
Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.
To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:
- Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5% - Life Assurance Cover - Free financial guidance and mortgage advice - Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving - Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active - Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting - Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers - Will Writing Service for future planning - Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support - Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs - Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community - Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts
…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

job.title: Retail Sales Advisor, Chester Store
job.html_description:
We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our Liverpool FC team as a Retail Sales Advisor at our Chester Store. 

We’re looking for passionate, customer-focused individuals who love working as part of a team. In this role, you’ll be at the heart of our in‑store experience, ensuring every visitor enjoys the highest possible standard of service and leaves feeling part of the LFC family. 

What will you be doing? 

  • Dealing with customer sales and refunds, ensuring a focus on customer care and the customer experience.
  • Till work including processing cash and card payments.
  • Gaining and building upon Product knowledge to provide advice and guidance on product selection to customers.
  • Basic Sales Management through driving the AVS, UPT and Conversion by offering add on sales and upselling.
  • Merchandising/display.
  • Stock shrinkage and cash flow controls.
  • Supporting in receiving and storing the delivery of large amounts of stock.

Who are we looking for? 

To be successful, you must have recent retail store and customer service experience in a customer facing role and can demonstrate this important skill set, with excellent numeracy and literacy skills. Having strong interpersonal and communication skills, you will be confident in delivering excellent customer service and working as part of a team. A retail and/or customer service qualifications would be advantageous. You will be able to work flexible hours. 

Why should you apply? 

This position is offering a permanent contract 16 hours per week on a 5 from 7-day basis, including evenings, weekends and matchdays. Your main base will be our Chester store. 

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Casual VIP Hospitality & Bar Staff
job.html_description:
Here at Liverpool Football Club, we are recruiting experienced Hospitality & Bar staff to deliver exceptional service within our VIP and premium lounges on matchdays, on a casual basis.

This role is suited to hospitality professionals who are confident working in premium, fast-paced environments.

What will you be doing?

  • Delivering high-quality table service within VIP and corporate hospitality lounges.
  • Providing professional bar service including draft, bottled and premium beverages.
  • Serving plated meals, set menus and drinks efficiently during peak service periods.
  • Supporting smooth and coordinated service during high-volume matchday intervals.
  • Maintaining exceptional presentation standards across dining and bar areas.
  • Demonstrating strong allergen awareness and product knowledge.

Who are we looking for?

  • Previous experience within a premium hospitality environment, such as fine dining restaurants, corporate hospitality, VIP lounges or stadium hospitality.
  • Experience working within high-volume, time sensitive service environments (e.g. matchdays, large scale events, peak restaurant service).
  • Confident providing professional bar service including draft, bottled and premium beverages.
  • Strong understanding of service etiquette, guest engagement and presentation standards.
  • Demonstrable allergen awareness and food safety knowledge.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

job.title: Casual Match Day Stewards
job.html_description:
Here at Liverpool Football Club, we are looking for Casual Match Day Stewards to join our team.

As part of the recruitment process, shortlisted candidates will be invited to attend a Group Induction & Interview. During the induction, members of our Safety Team will introduce the role, outline key responsibilities, and provide essential information. This will be followed by individual 121 interviews to assess your suitability for the position.

Please submit your application for the role to be considered for invitation to a Group Induction & Interview session.

What will you be doing in the role?

  • Working as part of a team to provide a safe and secure environment for match day events.
  • Ensuring compliance with stadium regulations, keeping spectators safe and informed.
  • Responding effectively to any incidents of disorder with a calm and focused approach.
  • Offering support and guidance to spectators when needed, ensuring they feel welcome and informed.
  • Conducting personal searches of spectators entering the stadium to maintain security.
  • Documenting and reporting incidents, ensuring clear and accurate records are kept.

We are looking for people who:

  • Have strong communication skills who can confidently engage with supporters, even in challenging situations. 
  • Can handle conflict calmly and professionally.
  • Can write clear reports and possess a basic understanding of conflict management.
  • Are over 18, due to the nature of the role.

If you are someone who can bring experience, calm, and a professional approach to this important role, we would love to hear from you.

By applying for the position of Casual Match Day Steward with Liverpool Football Club, please be aware that this role requires the completion of a Level 2 Certificate in Spectator Safety (as per the Sports Grounds Safety Authority), which must be completed within 12 months from the date of your first induction training.

By submitting your application, you confirm that you can commit to completing the Level 2 Certificate in Spectator Safety within 12 months from your first induction training. As this qualification is essential for the role, if you are unable to complete it within this timeframe, we may not be able to progress your application.

This is a casual position, offering variable hours including evenings and weekends on a flexible assignment basis.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

job.title: Supervisor, DC Operations
job.html_description:
We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our Liverpool FC team as Supervisor in our Distribution Centre. 

Our warehouse is at the centre of our business, so we are looking for a highly motivated supervisor with the ability to establish and maintain positive relationships with people at all levels. You will be responsible for managing all day-to-day warehouse operations. 

Role Overview:

The Supervisor leads a team of warehouse operatives during their shift, ensuring safe, accurate, and timely completion of all operational tasks. They allocate work, monitor performance against KPIs, and coach colleagues on best practices and compliance. Supervisors are the first point of escalation for operational and personnel issues and function as a bridge between operatives and management. They enforce health, safety, and security standards, maintain stock accuracy through cycle counts and checks, and support continuous improvement initiatives to optimise efficiency and service quality on the floor.

Day-to-Day Operational Management

  • Lead the team during the shift to ensure all tasks (inbound, picking, packing, replenishment, outbound, returns, production/personalisation) are completed to standard and on time.
  • Allocate work to colleagues, balancing resources against workflow and priorities set by the Operations Manager.
  • Monitor progress throughout the shift, resolving issues quickly and escalating where required.
  • Conduct effective shift handovers, ensuring continuity of operations.

Shift Team Leadership

  • Directly manage a team of warehouse operatives, setting expectations for productivity, accuracy, and behaviour.
  • Provide clear briefings at the start of shifts, communicating priorities, safety reminders, and performance targets.
  • Motivate and support the team, recognising good performance and addressing underperformance.
  • Function as first point of escalation for team issues, escalating to Operations Manager when appropriate.

Stock Control & Accuracy

  • Ensure operatives follow correct processes for scanning, put away, picking, and replenishment.
  • Conduct spot checks and cycle count on shift to maintain stock accuracy.
  • Investigate and resolve immediate stock discrepancies, escalating patterns or key issues.
  • Monitor handling of damages, returns, and quarantined stock to ensure compliance.

Performance & Continuous Improvement

  • Monitor team KPIs in real time (pick/pack rates, accuracy, errors, throughput) and take corrective action where needed.
  • Identify process or workflow issues and suggest improvements to the Operations Manager.
  • Ensure colleagues follow standard operating procedures (SOPs) at all times.

People Management & Development

  • Support recruitment, onboarding, and training of warehouse operatives.
  • Coach colleagues on best practice and reinforce training on systems, equipment, and safety.
  • Monitor attendance, punctuality, and conduct within the team, raising issues to the Operations Manager.
  • Foster a culture of accountability, teamwork, and respect.

Health & Safety & Compliance

  • Ensure colleagues comply with H&S requirements: correct PPE, safe manual handling, and adherence to safe systems of work.
  • Conduct daily safety checks in work areas (housekeeping, equipment, walkways).
  • Immediately report and investigate accidents, near misses, or unsafe behaviours.
  • Enforce compliance with company policies, security rules, and loss-prevention measures.

Operational Communication

  • Deliver clear daily briefings and toolbox talks.
  • Maintain effective two-way communication between operatives and management.
  • Provide accurate reporting of shift activity, issues, and outcomes to the Operations Manager.

Who are we looking for?

To be successful in this role, you will have previous, recent experience of working within a DC environment in a similar position, with excellent knowledge of DC operations and business key trading periods. You will have an excellent understanding of Health and Safety regulations and managing health and safety on a day-to-day basis.  

You will be able to demonstrate strong leadership and the ability to drive a team to achieve targets. You’ll have a ‘can do attitude’ and the ability to work as part of a team, while also being able to use your own initiative and be able to make operational decisions. You will have excellent communication skills both written and verbal, with a high level of accuracy and keen eye for detail, with the ability to effectively prioritise a demanding workload and positive and flexible approach to new challenges. 

Why should you apply? 

This is a full-time role working 35 hours per week on a 5 from 7 days basis which will include weekend working. Your main base will be our Distribution Centre in Whiston. We offer on-site parking. 

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:
- Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5% - Life Assurance Cover - Free financial guidance and mortgage advice - Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving - Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active - Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting - Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers - Will Writing Service for future planning - Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support - Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs - Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community - Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts
…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf
At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.
Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.
#LFCJobs

job.title: Operational Administrator, Distribution Centre
job.html_description:
We have an exciting opportunity for an individual to join our Liverpool FC team as a Operational Administrator at our Distribution Centre. This role will be working side by side with our operational team and is a warehouse shop-floor based role.

In this role, you will provide vital administrative support to Supervisors and operational teams, ensuring smooth order processing, accurate reporting, and ongoing SLA compliance. You will investigate exceptions, monitor stock and operational data, compile reports, and work closely with internal and external stakeholders to resolve queries. You’ll also support team rostering, training, and onboarding activities, while contributing to process improvements across the operation. Your work will ensure data is accurate, compliant, and clearly communicated to management, helping to inform decisions and drive strong operational performance.

What will you be doing?

Order Processing & Administration

  • Process and release orders in line with operational schedules and SLAs.
  • Investigate order delays or exceptions, identifying root causes and escalating where needed.
  • Maintain accurate records and ensure data integrity across WMS/ERP systems.
  • Carrying out all operational administration tasks related to Retail Store deliveries.
  • Undertaking all operational administration tasks related to customer ecommerce orders and returns.  

Internal & External Support

  • Provide administrative support to retail stores, customer service, planning, finance, and other teams.
  • Respond to queries on orders, stock, and deliveries from internal stakeholders and external partners.
  • Prepare and distribute reports, dashboards, and operational metrics for management.
  • Stock & Data Management
  • Support stock administration, system updates, and discrepancy reconciliation.
  • Assist with cycle counts and inventory activities.
  • Monitor data accuracy and help maintain loss‑prevention processes.

Staffing & Training Support

  • Assist Supervisors with rostering, absence tracking, and shift planning.
  • Support onboarding and training of colleagues on systems and processes.
  • Act as a point of contact for administrative guidance.
  • Process Improvement & Compliance
  • Identify recurring issues and contribute to process improvements.
  • Support implementation of new systems or procedures.
  • Ensure administrative tasks comply with company policies, audit standards, and regulatory requirements.

Communication & Coordination

  • Maintain strong communication with operational teams and cross‑functional departments.
  • Provide timely updates on order exceptions, stock challenges, and admin bottlenecks.
  • Act as a liaison to ensure smooth and efficient information flow.

Who are we looking for?

To be successful, you will have proven experience and knowledge of working in a administrative position in a distribution centre environment or a similar setting. You will be analytical and with great troubleshooting skills when dealing with stock investigations. You will understand business key trading periods and WMS experience and knowledge ideally PVX and Netsuite. 

You will have good organisational skills with excellent attention to detail and the ability to work to and meet deadlines. You will have strong verbal and written communication skills with a "can do" approach and have the flexibility to work within our operational administration team. You will be able to work in a fast-paced environment and work well within a team environment.

Why should you apply?

This is a full-time perm position working 35 hours per week working any five days out of seven which will include regular weekend working. Your main base will be our Distribution centre.

Please note, this vacancy may close early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

To reward your hard work and commitment we offer a competitive salary, 25 days holiday (plus 8 bank holidays and the option to purchase up to an additional 5 days) and access to our benefits kit bag which includes but not limited to:

  • Contributory pension scheme matching up to 5.5%
  • Life Assurance Cover
  • Free financial guidance and mortgage advice
  • Car salary sacrifice scheme for affordable driving
  • Cycle to Work scheme to keep you active
  • Purchase season ticket loans for easier commuting
  • Exclusive shopping discounts & cashback with top retailers
  • Will Writing Service for future planning
  • Employee Assistance Programme for confidential support
  • Medicash Health Cash Plan for everyday healthcare needs
  • Volunteering opportunities to give back to the community
  • Special LFC perks – retail discounts, partner offers, free LFCTV GO access, and priority tickets for matches, events & concerts

…and much more! LFC Benefits.pdf

At Liverpool Football Club, we have an unwavering commitment to equality, diversity and inclusion and are always looking to making a positive difference in the communities that we operate within. We are proud of our achievements in this area; maintaining the Premier League Equality Standard Advanced Level, becoming a founding signatory of the Football Association’s Football Leadership Diversity Code and being recognised as a leader in this important area on and off the pitch. We take our responsibilities in this area seriously and through the work being done across the club, we are committed to increasing the diversity of our people and becoming an increasingly inclusive workplace for all. We are committed to hiring great people representative of diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills across our entire business. If you share our enthusiasm and passion for inclusivity, then we want to hear from you.

Liverpool FC is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and vulnerable adults and expects all Colleagues and Volunteers to share this commitment.

#LFCJobs

job.title: Performance Psychologist - Women’s Team (Casual Maternity Cover)
Senior Academy Scout
job.html_description:
Senior Academy Scout

Permanent 

Competitive Salary
Closing Date: 16th March 2026

We are the heartbeat of the city. Come and be a part of a long and proud history where we strive to be the best in everything we do, on and off the pitch. Bringing people and communities together, join the Newcastle United family as we embark on the next steps of our exciting journey.

About the role

We’re looking for a Senior Academy Scout to join our Academy Recruitment team, where you will operate within the Club’s established framework, player profiles and scouting methodology to support informed and effective decision‑making across Talent Identification. You’ll work closely with the Academy Recruitment Director, Academy Scouting Director and Scout Analysts to identify academy‑level players across the UK and Europe, while managing three Regional Scouts across the British Isles.

This is a pivotal role in a highly competitive recruitment environment, requiring deep understanding of development pathways, player traits, scouting insights and emerging talent trends.

Your impact

In this role, you will:

*         Lead the identification and monitoring of academy‑level players across the UK and Europe.

*         Manage Regional Scouts (NI, ROI & Scotland) and ensure structured, high‑quality reporting and six‑weekly cross‑checks.

*         Provide detailed Talent ID insights, maintain accurate coverage records, and support decision‑ready processes for high potential academy players who fit the Newcastle United players profiles.

*         Produce high‑level reports for senior Academy recruitment leaders, outlining opportunities and key emerging players.

*         Influence decision‑making as part of the core recruitment group, ensuring we remain ahead of competitors.

*         Support analysts with pitching videos and contribute to structured workflows and data‑driven insights.

*         Travel internationally as required to assess target players

You’ll play an active leadership role, ensuring CPD is in place for your team and ensuring compliance with people processes and safeguarding expectations.

What you’ll bring

*         Significant experience within a high‑end Premier League recruitment environment.

*         Detailed football knowledge including game models, playing styles and technical/tactical/physical/psychological player traits.

*         Strong discipline in following workflows and excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

*         Experience of key industry systems (e.g., Wyscout, Hudl, Sportscode, Studio).

*         Ability to work independently and remotely while maintaining team cohesion.

*         Strong attention to detail and the ability to make well‑substantiated recommendations.

*         Understanding of how data contributes to recruitment decisions.

*         Growth mindset, adaptability and comfort with evolving recruitment trends.

*         Ability to meet strict deadlines with professionalism and composure.

*         FA Level 3 Talent ID (or equivalent).

Why choose us?

We offer a fantastic range of rewards including, participation in our non‑contractual employee bonus scheme, NUFC life assurance, free parking, discounts at Shearers Bar and the Club Shop, access to the Helping Hand wellbeing platform, pension contributions, free lunch and refreshments, plus salary‑sacrifice benefits including tech, car and cycle‑to‑work schemes.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment where everyone can be their authentic self.

Safeguarding

We are dedicated to providing a safe environment where everyone is heard, valued and able to thrive. This role requires an Enhanced DBS with Barred List Check. Safeguarding is everyone’s responsibility, and all colleagues are expected to operate in line with Club policies and best practice.

How to apply

To access the full job description for this role please click here.

For further information on this role and about the club and our values please visit our careers page.

Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates.

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate.

job.title: Lead Recruitment Analyst
job.html_description:
Lead Recruitment Analyst

Newcastle United Women

Permanent

Newcastle Upon Tyne

Competitive Salary

Closing date:-14/03/2026

We are the heartbeat of the city. Come and be a part of a long and proud history where we strive to be the best in everything we do, on and off the pitch. Bringing the people and communities together, join the Newcastle United Womens Family, as we embark on the next steps of our exciting journey.

About the role

As Lead Recruitment Analyst, you’ll drive our player recruitment strategy by linking live and video scouting with robust data insights. You will manage the operational and analytical engine of the department—curating evidence, creating clarity, and delivering recommendations that align with the vision of the Head of Recruitment & Analysis and the Director of Women’s Football.

Key Responsibilities

  • Player Identification & Scouting
  • Conduct video and live scouting across senior and emerging markets.
  • Produce detailed scouting reports and presentations aligned with club philosophy.
  • Build and manage positional scouting lists.
  • Perform due diligence and background checks on potential recruits.
  • Data & Performance Analysis
  • Use Sportscode, telestration, and video tools to create clips and evidence.
  • Integrate performance data into scouting reports.
  • Maintain a video and data library of target players.
  • Market Intelligence & Benchmarking
  • Research salaries, bonuses, and transfer trends for negotiations.
  • Monitor domestic and international markets for opportunities.
  • Operations & Process Management
  • Manage/ information flow across the scouting network.

Skills & Experience

  • Significant experience in professional football recruitment/scouting.
  • Expertise in video analysis platforms (Sportscode, telestration).
  • Strong data literacy and ability to translate insights into recommendations.
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills
  • Organised, detail-oriented, and collaborative.
  • Flexibility for travel and live match attendance.
  • Background: Substantial experience in professional football recruitment, analysis or scouting.
  • Technical craft: Strong expertise with video analysis platforms (e.g., Sportscode, telestration) and a high level of data literacy—able to translate insights into clear recommendations.
  • Communication: Excellent written and verbal skills, with the ability to produce concise reports and impactful presentations.
  • Ways of working: Organised, collaborative, and detail‑driven; flexible to attend live matches and travel domestically/internationally as required.

Qualifications

Essential

Degree in Sports Science, Performance Analysis or related field.

FA Talent ID Level 2.

Desirable

Master’s degree in related field.

FA Talent ID Level 3.

Why choose us?

We’ve got a range of great benefits and rewards, from flexible ways of working, participation in our non-contractual employee bonus scheme, NUFC life assurance, free parking, discount at Shearers Bar and the Club Shop, Helping Hand – where you can access free GP appointments, Wellbeing Resources, Legal and Financial Support, pension contribution, free lunch and the best part, free tea and coffee. In addition, we run a salary sacrifice scheme which includes, tech, car, cycle to work and many more.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment in which everyone can be their authentic self and is enabled to achieve their full potential.

Safeguarding

We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

How to apply

To access the full job description for this role please Click here

Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates.

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate.

job.title: CRM Manager
job.html_description:
CRM Manager

Permanent, Full Time

Newcastle upon Tyne

Competitive Salary

Be part of something bigger. Turn data into smart decisions - personalising every experience at scale. 

We are the heartbeat of the city. A Club with bold ambitions on a global stage and deep roots in our community. If you’re driven to raise the bar and make an impact, you’ll find the platform, the people and the pride to do your best work here.

This is a great opportunity to develop, learn and make a real contribution in a fast paced and exciting environment.

About the role

We’re looking for a CRM Manager to play a key role in delivering the Club’s global CRM strategy focused on fan acquisition, engagement and lifetime value. Working alongside the CRM lead, you will take responsibility for translating strategic objectives into actionable plans and ensuring CRM activity is delivered to a high standard across all stages of the fan lifecycle.

You will own the end-to-end delivery of CRM campaigns from briefing through to audience creation, execution and performance analysis. You will also lead the development and optimisation of automated lifecycle journeys that nurture fans throughout their relationship with the Club. As a central point of contact for stakeholders, you will ensure CRM activity aligns with business priorities and enhances the fan experience.

Hands on experience with ESPs and CDPs is essential. Bloomreach experience is ideal, but Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Braze or similar platforms are also valuable.

What you’ll do

*        Help shape and deliver the Club’s CRM strategy focused on fan acquisition, engagement and commercial performance.

*        Translate strategic objectives in a multichannel CRM plan, ensuring alignment across key stakeholders and teams.

*        Own the end-to-end delivery of CRM campaigns including briefing, audience segmentation, build, QA, execution and analysis.

*        Lead the creation, implementation and optimisation of automated lifecycle journeys that drive onboarding, engagement, retention and reactivation.

*        Maintain high standards of data governance ensuring compliance with GDPR and UK data protection requirements.

*        Drive data capture initiatives across all touchpoints, working cross‑functionally to track KPIs and test new ways to improve acquisition.

*        Use insights and reporting to optimise CRM performance and improve fan experience.

*        Manage CRM database hygiene practices and integration of new data sources

About you

*        Demonstrable experience working in CRM, lifecycle marketing, with a strong track record of delivery effective campaigns.

*        Comfortable taking ownership of projects and leading CRM activity from planning through to execution and review.

*        Confident working with customer data and building audiences using CRM tools such as Bloomreach, Salesforce Marketing Cloud or Braze (Bloomreach preferred but not essential).

*        Strong understanding of how to create and improve lifecycle strategies that drive conversion, engagement and retention.

*        Highly organised with the ability to manage several projects at once and adapt to changing priorities.

*        Skilled at working with different teams and building strong relationships with stakeholders at all levels.

*        Excellent written and verbal communication skills with a high attention to detail.

*        Strong understanding of GDPR and UK data protection requirements.

*        Experience managing or developing team members.

The team

You’ll be part of a friendly, supportive and dedicated team who will help you settle in and succeed. We value curiosity, collaboration and a positive mindset. You’ll work closely with colleagues across Marketing, Digital, Retail and Analytics, as well as external partners.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and maintain an inclusive environment where everyone can be their authentic self and achieve their full potential.

Safeguarding

We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

How to apply  

To access the full job description for this role please click here.

For further information on this role and about the club please visit our careers page. 

Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates. 

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate. 

Right to work

This position is UK‑based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All offers are subject to background checks, including references, DBS where applicable, and verification of eligibility to work. Please note we are unable to sponsor applicants without existing right‑to‑work status.

* Help shape and deliver the Club’s CRM strategy focused on fan acquisition, engagement and commercial performance.
* Translate strategic objectives in a multichannel CRM plan, ensuring alignment across key stakeholders and teams.
* Own the end-to-end delivery of CRM campaigns including briefing, audience segmentation, build, QA, execution and analysis.
* Lead the creation, implementation and optimisation of automated lifecycle journeys that drive onboarding, engagement, retention and reactivation.
* Maintain high standards of data governance ensuring compliance with GDPR and UK data protection requirements.
* Drive data capture initiatives across all touchpoints, working cross‑functionally to track KPIs and test new ways to improve acquisition.
* Use insights and reporting to optimise CRM performance and improve fan experience.
* Manage CRM database hygiene practices and integration of new data sources
About you
* Demonstrable experience working in CRM, lifecycle marketing, with a strong track record of delivery effective campaigns.
* Comfortable taking ownership of projects and leading CRM activity from planning through to execution and review.
* Confident working with customer data and building audiences using CRM tools such as Bloomreach, Salesforce Marketing Cloud or Braze (Bloomreach preferred but not essential).
* Strong understanding of how to create and improve lifecycle strategies that drive conversion, engagement and retention.
* Highly organised with the ability to manage several projects at once and adapt to changing priorities.
* Skilled at working with different teams and building strong relationships with stakeholders at all levels.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills with a high attention to detail.
* Strong understanding of GDPR and UK data protection requirements.
* Experience managing or developing team members.
The team
You’ll be part of a friendly, supportive and dedicated team who will help you settle in and succeed. We value curiosity, collaboration and a positive mindset. You’ll work closely with colleagues across Marketing, Digital, Retail and Analytics, as well as external partners.
United As One
We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and maintain an inclusive environment where everyone can be their authentic self and achieve their full potential.
Safeguarding
We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.
How to apply
To access the full job description for this role please click here.
For further information on this role and about the club please visit our careers page.
Please apply as soon as possible as this vacancy may close early should we receive a high volume of suitable candidates.
Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited / speculative candidate details or applications. Any candidates supplied, unless formally requested, will be taken as a direct / free candidate.
Right to work
This position is UK‑based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All offers are subject to background checks, including references, DBS where applicable, and verification of eligibility to work. Please note we are unable to sponsor applicants without existing right‑to‑work status.

job.title: Academy Scouting Operations Manager
job.html_description:
Job Title: Academy Scouting Operations Manager

Location: Remote

Contract Type: Permanent

Salary: Competitive

Closing Date: 22nd March 2026

We are the heartbeat of the city. Come and be a part of a long and proud history where we strive to be the best in everything we do, on and off the pitch. Bringing the people and communities together, join the Newcastle United Family, as we embark on the next steps of our exciting journey.

About the role

We’re looking for a highly organised and strategically minded Academy Scouting Operations Manager to lead the operational planning, coordination, and delivery of all scouting activity across the Academy’s European scouting arm. In this role, you will work within the MCO’s established framework and methodology to drive scouting workflows, manage key administrative processes, and ensure the accurate assessment and prioritisation of scouting data.

Your impact

In this role, you will oversee the operational engine behind our European academy scouting network. You will organise scouting workflows, create weekly and monthly schedules, and act as the key point of contact for scout requests. You’ll maintain schedule planners, assign fixtures and reports, manage databases and squad lists, and ensure all scouting information is captured accurately and efficiently.

About the team

You will work within the Academy Recruitment Department, collaborating closely with the Academy Recruitment Director, Academy Scouting Director, international regional scouts, and the UK scouting team.

Why choose us?

We’ve got a range of great benefits and rewards, including participation in our non-contractual employee bonus scheme, NUFC life assurance, free parking, discounts, wellbeing support, pension contribution, free lunch and refreshments, and salary sacrifice schemes.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all.

Safeguarding

This role requires an enhanced criminal records check (BLC) due to responsibilities relating to young people and adults at risk.

How to apply

To view the full job description for this role, please click here.

Please apply via our careers page. Early applications are encouraged as the vacancy may close early.

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited candidate details. Any candidates submitted without request will be considered direct applicants.

job.title: Host Families
job.location: Newcastle upon Tyne Discipline: Football Operations Job type: Casual Job ref: 008462 Published: 3 days ago Expiry date: 15 Mar 2026 20:00
job.html_description:
Could you provide a welcoming home for the next generation of Newcastle United stars?

 

We’re looking for Host Families to support our young players as they move to Newcastle United from outside the North East.

 

Why Become a Host Family?

 

*        Provide a stable, safe, and welcoming home

*        Play a vital role in a player’s growth on and off the pitch

*        Support their academic and personal development

*        Receive competitive remuneration

 

Family is at the heart of Newcastle United. We know our players perform at their best when they feel happy and secure at home.

 

We offer both short-term (1–2 nights) and full-time (7 nights per week) placements for players aged 14–21.

 

If you can offer a nurturing environment and want to make a real impact, we’d love to hear from you!

 

What we’re looking for:

 

We’re seeking Host Families in Newcastle upon Tyne (postcodes NE7, NE6, NE2, NE3, NE12), located close to our education partner or the Academy. You should be able to provide high-quality accommodation, ideally in a family environment.

 

Requirements:

 

*        Provide an evening meal, breakfast (for school-age players)

*        Access to laundry facilities and Wi-Fi

*        A safe and suitable environment for the player

*        Private bedroom with a double bed

*        Full or part-time placements available year-round to align with the football season

*        Willingness to accommodate trialists and provide holiday cover when needed

 

You’ll be able to:

 

*        Create a welcoming, homely environment for young players

*        Teach essential independent living skills like cooking, cleaning, and self-care

*        Provide meals following nutritional guidance from the Academy

*        Ensure accommodation is close to local amenities with good transport links to the Academy

*        Report any welfare concerns to the Academy’s Safeguarding and Wellbeing team

*        Integrate players into family life as much as they feel comfortable

*        Accommodate regular inspections and visits during the season

*        Build trusted relationships through open communication

*        Adapt to the flexible schedule of young players throughout the football season

*        Promote and demonstrate a strong anti-discriminatory practice

 

It would be great if you have:

 

*        Experience working with young people

*        Understanding of the emotional impact and well-being challenges young athletes face

*        A child-focused approach

*        The capacity to accommodate more than one player

 

We welcome applications from all sections of the community. In particular, we encourage candidates from Black, Asian and other minority ethnic backgrounds to apply, as these groups are currently under-represented in this role. If you're ready to offer a supportive home and make a lasting impact, we'd love to hear from you! 

 

To express an interest and find out more information please email Callum.Thompson@nufc.co.uk detailing the following:

 

1)      Your full name and current address

2)      Current occupation

3)      Whether you are interested in offering a full-time placement or intermittent support for trialists

4)      Your current property situation including availability of a private bedroom / number of rooms available

5)      Why you would like to become a host family in no more than 250 words

job.title: Cleaning Supervisor
job.location: Newcastle upon Tyne Discipline: Venue Operations Job type: Permanent Job ref: 008542 Published: 2 days ago Expiry date: 24 Mar 2026 08:00
job.html_description:
Cleaning Supervisor

Newcastle upon Tyne

Permanent

Competitive Salary

Closing Date: 24th March 2026

Keep our sites safe, sharp and efficient - ready for unforgettable moments. 

We are the heartbeat of the city. Come and be a part of a long and proud history where we strive to be the best in everything we do, on and off the pitch. Bringing the people and communities together, join the Newcastle United Family, as we embark on the next steps of our exciting journey.

About the role

We’re looking for a committed and experienced Cleaning Supervisor to help ensure our stadium remains a clean, safe, and welcoming environment for supporters, staff, and visitors. In this role, you will uphold the highest cleaning standards across all areas of St. James’ Park, supporting daily operations and leading by example with a helpful, approachable, and professional attitude.

Your impact

As a Cleaning Supervisor, you will train and induct new staff in C.O.S.H.H. and BICS-aligned methods, undertake monthly audits, and supervise large teams during matchday and event cleaning operations. You will support event organisation, complete administrative tasks, and provide exceptional customer service. You'll liaise with clients, manage equipment needs, carry out minor repairs, replace dispensers, respond to emergency call-outs, and ensure staff follow all Health & Safety procedures.

About the team

You will be part of the Cleaning team within Venue Operations, working closely with the Cleaning Manager and supervising Cleaning staff to deliver a high‑quality and efficient service across the stadium.

Location

This role is based at St. James’ Park, with occasional work required at other sites. Flexibility is essential, especially during matchdays, evenings, weekends, and public holidays.

Why choose us?

We’ve got a range of great benefits and rewards, participation in our non-contractual employee bonus scheme, NUFC life assurance, free parking, discounts at Shearers Bar and the Club Shop, Helping Hand wellbeing support, pension contribution, free lunch, tea, and coffee, and salary sacrifice schemes.

United As One

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all.

Safeguarding

We place high value on safeguarding and the welfare of everyone we engage with. This role does not require a criminal records check.

How to apply

To view the full job description, please click here.

Early applications are encouraged as the vacancy may close early if we receive a large volume of applicants.

This position is UK based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All job offers are conditional upon background checks, including reference history, DBS checks, and verification of your eligibility to work. Please note, we do not sponsor applicants without the right to work in the UK.  

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited candidate details. Any candidates submitted without request will be considered direct applicants.

job.title: Executive Assistants New
job.location: Newcastle upon Tyne Discipline: Senior Leadership Job type: Permanent Contact name: Test Test Contact email: neil.gatenby@nufc.co.uk Job ref: 008602 Published: about 7 hours ago Expiry date: 26 Mar 2026 12:00
job.html_description:
Executive Assistants – 1 x Permanent and 1 x Fixed Term for 12 months   

Permanent / Fixed Term

Location: Newcastle Upon Tyne

Competitive Salary

Be part of something bigger. Behind every big decision — you’re part of the Team making it happen

We are the heartbeat of the city.  A club with bold ambitions on a global stage and deep roots in our community. If you’re driven to raise the bar and make an impact, you’ll find the platform, the people and the pride to do your best work here.

This is a great opportunity to develop, learn and make a real contribution in a fast-paced and exciting environment.

What you’ll do

You’ll be the steady hand supporting our Executive Team — keeping plans tight, conversations flowing, and decisions moving.

If you’re at your best when things move fast — when you’re bringing order, clarity and calm to a busy day — this is your chance to make a real difference. We’re looking for an Executive Assistant who sees what’s needed before it’s asked for. Someone who backs themselves, brings energy, and helps us keep pushing forward.

One role will support our Chief Operating Officer and our Chief Financial Officer with all of things that allow them to focus on delivering our strategic aims. The second role will work closely with the broader business and People and Talent Team to ensure that our new C-suite recruits have a first class introduction to the Club, with the opportunity to work with the new team longer term.

You’ll take ownership of:

  • Managing communications and inboxes with confidence and discretion
  • Coordinating complex diaries across senior stakeholders
  • Preparing clear, high‑quality materials for executive and board meetings
  • Organising travel and expenses
  • Keeping documents and systems organised and accurate
  • Building strong relationships inside and outside the Club
  • Supporting wider projects, including minute taking and office coordination
  • Handling every detail in line with our values and expectations

What You’ll Bring

  • Significant experience supporting C‑Suite or senior leaders in an Executive Assistant / PA role.
  • Strong communication skills and a calm, adaptable approach
  • Confidence managing shifting priorities
  • Excellent MS Office skills
  • Problem‑solving instincts and a proactive mindset
  • Professionalism under pressure

It Helps If You Also Have

  • Experience in an elite sporting environment
  • Understanding of data protection
  • Experience working with diverse teams

Essential:

  • A‑Levels or equivalent experience
  • GCSE English and Maths (C or above)

Desirable:

  • Project management qualification
  • Microsoft Office certifications
  • ACEA qualification

The team

 

You’ll be part of a friendly, supportive and dedicated team who will help you settle in and succeed. We value curiosity, teamwork and a positive approach.
To access the full job description please click here.

United As One.

 

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment in which everyone can be their authentic self and is enabled to achieve their full potential.

 

Safeguarding

We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

 

This position is UK based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All job offers are conditional upon background checks, including reference history, DBS checks, and verification of your eligibility to work. Please note, we do not sponsor applicants without the right to work in the UK.

 

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited candidate details. Any candidates submitted without request will be considered direct .

job.title: Legend Engagement Coordinator New
job.location: Newcastle upon Tyne Discipline: Commercial Job type: Permanent Job ref: 008622 Published: about 4 hours ago Expiry date: 26 Mar 2026 08:00
job.html_description:
Legend Engagement Coordinator

Permanent – Full Time

Newcastle upon Tyne

Competitive Salary

Be part of something bigger.

Champion our supporters - solve problems and make every interaction count.

We are the heartbeat of the city. A club with bold ambitions on a global stage and deep roots in our community. If you're driven to raise the bar and make an impact, you'll find the platform, the people and the pride to do your best work here.

This is a great opportunity to develop, learn and make a real contribution in a fast-paced and exciting environment.

About the role

Develop and deliver an annual programme of initiatives that celebrate Newcastle United’s legends and ambassadors, ensuring they feel valued, connected, and integral to the club’s ongoing story. Coordinate face‑to‑face and digital activities and communications that ensure meaningful relationships between legends and ambassadors, and the wider club community, working independently to create memorable experiences. Collaborate across departments including Communications, Hospitality, Marketing, Retail and Football Operations to provide seamless support and recognition, embedding excellence in every interaction.

Role Requirements

*         Perform duties with due regard to club policies and procedures and legislative requirements at all times.

*         Ensure implementation of the club’s health & safety, safeguarding, welfare and equality policies to create a safe working environment for all.

*         Ensure working practices comply with relevant legislation, including data protection/GDPR requirements.

*         Undertake continuous professional development and any additional training required.

What you’ll do

*         Create an initial Legends & Ambassador Engagement Plan of activities and events that strengthens the connection between former legends, ambassadors, and Newcastle United.

*         Deliver an annual calendar of face‑to‑face and digital initiatives and events that ensure our legends feel valued and included in the life of the club.

*         Map engagement journeys for legends and ambassadors, ensuring every interaction is meaningful, respectful, and enhances their sense of belonging.

*         Maintain regular contact with legends and ambassadors via phone, email, or other appropriate methods to build strong professional relationships.

*         Manage administration of program delivery, keeping detailed reporting of engagement activity and maintaining accurate records.

*         Work with the internal marketing team to create and manage digital, print and multimedia marketing assets aligned with brand guidelines and heritage storytelling.

*         Evaluate the success of initiatives and events, producing feedback reports and identifying opportunities for improvement.

*         Coordinate and record ambassador appearances across departments, ensuring seamless organisation and strong club representation.

*         Deliver other engagement activities as required to strengthen stakeholder relationships.

*         Promote the Newcastle United brand and ethos in a professional, strong and positive manner.

*         Any other reasonable duties.

The team

You’ll be part of a friendly, supportive and dedicated team who will help you settle in and succeed. We value curiosity, teamwork and a positive approach.

United As One.

We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment in which everyone can be their authentic self and is enabled to achieve their full potential.

Safeguarding

We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.

To view the full job description, please click here.

This position is UK based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All job offers are conditional upon background checks, including reference history, DBS checks, and verification of your eligibility to work. Please note, we do not sponsor applicants without the right to work in the UK.

Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited candidate details. Any candidates submitted without request will be considered direct applicants.

* Evaluate the success of initiatives and events, producing feedback reports and identifying opportunities for improvement.
* Coordinate and record ambassador appearances across departments, ensuring seamless organisation and strong club representation.
* Deliver other engagement activities as required to strengthen stakeholder relationships.
* Promote the Newcastle United brand and ethos in a professional, strong and positive manner.
* Any other reasonable duties.
The team
You’ll be part of a friendly, supportive and dedicated team who will help you settle in and succeed. We value curiosity, teamwork and a positive approach.
United As One.
We’re committed to equality, diversity and inclusion and believe in equal opportunities for all. We recognise that the diversity of our people is one of our greatest strengths. We work together to reflect the communities we serve and to maintain an inclusive environment in which everyone can be their authentic self and is enabled to achieve their full potential.
Safeguarding
We’re committed to being a place where everyone is safe, heard, valued and able to thrive so we place high value on the safeguarding and welfare of everyone we engage with.
To view the full job description, please click here.
This position is UK based and requires proof of the right to live and work in the UK. All job offers are conditional upon background checks, including reference history, DBS checks, and verification of your eligibility to work. Please note, we do not sponsor applicants without the right to work in the UK.
Recruitment Agencies – please note that we do not accept unsolicited candidate details. Any candidates submitted without request will be considered direct applicants.

job.title: Host Family Provider
job.location: Within three miles (15 minutes), easy access to The Nigel Doughty Academy, Nottingham, NG2 7SR
job.html_description:
Academy · The Academy and Training Ground · Fully Remote

Host Family Provider

Contract: Temporary engagement

Location: Within three miles (15 minutes), easy access to The Nigel Doughty Academy, Nottingham, NG2 7SR

Working Arrangements: Opportunities to host on a full-time or part-time/ad-hoc basis

About the Role:

We are recruiting for host families who can provide accommodation for our Academy players to support them living away from home.  This is a unique and rewarding opportunity to make a real difference to their experience and provide vital support to the players as they develop as young people alongside their football.

Key Tasks and Responsibilities:

  • Provide a homely and friendly environment for our young people
  • Support with the development of our young people in your care with their independent living and self-care skills
  • Include our young people in family life as much as they feel comfortable with
  • Provide access to a private bedroom, bathroom, laundry facilities, and communal areas of the house
  • Provide a nutritious evening meal 
  • Report any welfare concerns to the Safeguarding and Welfare team should they occur.
  • Allow access from the Club for regular inspection and visits throughout the season.
  • Adhere to any further and ongoing requirements of Host Families, as outlined in the application process, guidance will be provided by the Club on this. 

What are we looking for:

  • Provide a warm, welcoming and safe environment for our young person
  • Provide the young person with their own private bedroom and relevant amenities 
  • Excellent communication and listening skills
  • Ability to cope with the young person's flexible schedule throughout the football season
  • Previous experience of working with young people
  • Ability to promote and demonstrate anti-discriminatory practice 

As part of the recruitment process the Club would need to conduct a number of home visits to determine suitability and ensure the home meets the requirements for hosting young people within the Academy.


Forest For Everyone

At Nottingham Forest Football Club we are committed to creating an environment where differences are respected, individuality is celebrated, everyone feels valued, supported, and included. Our goal is to ensure every member of our team can be themselves, contribute fully, and reach their potential without barriers.

Our Commitment to Equality, Diversity, Inclusion and Mental Wellbeing

As an equal opportunities employer, we welcome applications from talented people of all backgrounds regardless of age, disability, gender identity or reassignment, marital or civil partnership status, pregnancy or maternity, race, religion or belief, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by law.

We continue to develop policies and practices that promote equality, diversity, inclusion and mental wellbeing across our workforce. To help us measure progress and hold ourselves accountable, we ask all applicants to provide accurate diversity information during the recruitment process.

Disability Confident Committed Employer

We are proud to be a Disability Confident Committed employer, taking positive steps to attract, recruit, and support disabled people. As part of this commitment, we work hard to ensure our recruitment processes are fair, inclusive, and accessible to everyone. We are committed to offering interviews for disabled applicants who meet the essential criteria for a role and provide reasonable adjustments at every stage from the application process through to ongoing support in the workplace.

If you are likely to meet the definition of being a "disabled person" according to the Equality Act 2010, you may be eligible to apply for an interview via the Disability Confident Scheme. Please indicate this in your application form. The information you share with us about your health or disability will not be used in recruitment selections.

Our Commitment to Safeguarding

As part of the Club’s commitment to safeguarding, the successful applicant may be required to undergo a Disclosure Barring Service (“DBS”) check or an enhanced DBS check. The results of a DBS check will be considered on an individual basis, and the Club will act in a proportionate manner when deciding whether or not to proceed with the appointment.

An FA Enhanced DBS disclosure is required for all relevant new appointments to the Club. Individuals will be asked to declare any convictions, cautions or reprimands, warnings or bind-overs that may have been incurred. The Club reserves the right to carry out risk assessments where content has been disclosed on a DBS disclosure if it feels that it is relevant to the job requirements. Failure to satisfy the Club and the PL's safeguarding requirements may lead to the job offer being withdrawn or, if they have already started employment, summary termination of the employment.

Health and Safety

At Nottingham Forest Football Club, we are committed to ensuring a safe and healthy working environment for all employees. In accordance with the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974, you are required to take reasonable care for your own health and safety and that of others who may be affected by your actions or omissions at work. You must also cooperate with the Club's health and safety policies and procedures. Should you identify any health and safety concerns, please report them promptly to your manager or a member of the Health and Safety/Facilities team.

job.title: Casual Netball Community Coaches
job.html_description:
Forest Netball · The City Ground

Casual Netball Community Coaches

Job Title: Casual Netball Community Coaches

Location: Staffordshire, Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, and Lincolnshire

Job Type: Part-time / Casual Hours

Job Summary: Nottingham Forest Netball is seeking passionate and dedicated Community Coaches . The ideal candidates will be responsible for coaching and developing netball skills in various communities within Staffordshire, Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, and Lincolnshire. This role involves working with players of all ages and abilities, promoting the sport, and encouraging participation in a fun and supportive environment.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Plan and deliver high-quality netball coaching sessions to players of all ages and abilities.
  • Foster a positive and inclusive environment that encourages participation and enjoyment of netball.
  • Develop and implement training programs that cater to the individual needs of players.
  • Monitor and evaluate player progress, providing constructive feedback to help them improve.
  • Ensure the safety and well-being of all participants during coaching sessions.
  • Promote Nottingham Forest Netball and its programs within local communities.
  • Assist in organising and running local netball events, tournaments, and competitions.
  • Maintain equipment and ensure it is used safely and effectively.
  • Attend CPD events organised by Nottingham Forest.

Qualifications and Experience:

  • Netball coaching qualification for Head Coach (LEVEL 2 or ABOVE)
  • Netball coaching qualification for Assistant Coach (LEVEL 1 or ABOVE)
  • Previous coaching experience, preferably within a community setting.
  • Excellent communication.
  • Ability to inspire and motivate players of all ages and abilities.
  • Strong organisational and time management skills.
  • Commitment to promoting equality, diversity, and inclusion in sports.
  • Valid driver's license and access to a vehicle for travel between locations.
  • First Aid certification
  • Safeguarding certification

Desirable:

  • Experience working with young people or in educational settings.
  • Familiarity with the local netball community in Staffordshire, Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, or Lincolnshire.

Benefits:

  • Opportunities for professional development and training.
  • Flexible working hours.
  • The chance to be part of a dynamic and supportive team.


Our Commitment to Equality, Diversity & Inclusion

Nottingham Forest Football Club is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all suitably qualified persons, regardless of age, disability, gender, gender reassignment, marital / civil partnership status, pregnancy / maternity leave, race, religion/belief, sexual orientation, or any other legally protected characteristic.

Nottingham Forest Football Club is a signatory of the FA’s Football Leadership Diversity Code. We are committed to developing policies to promote equal opportunities and diversity within our workforce. To monitor our progress against these commitments, we require all applicants to provide accurate diversity data during the application process.

Our Commitment to Safeguarding

As part of the Club’s commitment to safeguarding, the successful applicant may be required to undergo a Disclosure Barring Service (“DBS”) check or an enhanced DBS check. The results of a DBS check will be considered on an individual basis, and the Club will act in a proportionate manner when deciding whether or not to proceed with the appointment.

An FA Enhanced DBS disclosure is required for all relevant new appointments to the Club. Individuals will be asked to declare any convictions, cautions or reprimands, warnings or bind-overs that may have been incurred. The Club reserves the right to carry out risk assessments where content has been disclosed on a DBS disclosure if it feels that it is relevant to the job requirements. Failure to satisfy the Club and the PL's safeguarding requirements may lead to the job offer being withdrawn or, if they have already started employment, summary termination of the employment.
